```{r}
# Significance stars for convenience (not currently added to the table)
sig_code <- function(p) {
  ifelse(is.na(p), "",
         ifelse(p < 0.001, "***",
                ifelse(p < 0.01,  "**",
                       ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", ""))))
}

# Safely extract a single row from a summary table by (partial) rowname match.
# Returns numeric vector of that row; NAs if not found.
row_pick <- function(tab, pattern) {
  if (is.null(tab)) return(rep(NA_real_, 4))
  rn  <- rownames(tab)
  hit <- which(grepl(pattern, rn, fixed = TRUE))
  if (length(hit) < 1) return(rep(NA_real_, ncol(tab)))
  as.numeric(tab[hit[1], ])
}

# `%||%` helper: use RHS if LHS is NULL (keeps code concise below)
`%||%` <- function(x, y) if (is.null(x)) y else x

# ----------------- build one-row summary per family ---------------

one_binom_row <- function(fam, mod) {
  # Skip gracefully if model is missing
  if (is.null(mod)) return(NULL)

  # mgcv summary is the main source of fit stats
  s   <- summary(mod)
  n   <- length(residuals(mod))                         # sample size used
  aic <- tryCatch(AIC(mod), error = function(e) NA_real_)

  # ---- Parametric terms: season effect (Autumn vs Spring) ----
  # Works for both $p.table (typical) or $parametric.anova (alternative)
  ptab      <- s$p.table %||% s$parametric.anova
  par_vals  <- row_pick(ptab, "seasonAutumn")           # [Estimate, SE, (z/t), p]
  est_season <- par_vals[1]
  se_season  <- par_vals[2]
  p_season   <- if (ncol(ptab) >= 4) par_vals[4] else NA_real_

  # ---- Smooth terms: edf & p for the seasonal time smooths and site RE ----
  stab <- s$s.table
  # Convention: column 1 is edf; last column is p-value (Chi.sq/F p)
  edf_time_S <- if (!is.null(stab)) {
    r <- grep("s\\(decimal_date\\).*Spring", rownames(stab))
    if (length(r)) stab[r[1], 1] else NA_real_
  } else NA_real_
  p_time_S <- if (!is.null(stab)) {
    r <- grep("s\\(decimal_date\\).*Spring", rownames(stab))
    if (length(r)) stab[r[1], ncol(stab)] else NA_real_
  } else NA_real_

  edf_time_A <- if (!is.null(stab)) {
    r <- grep("s\\(decimal_date\\).*Autumn", rownames(stab))
    if (length(r)) stab[r[1], 1] else NA_real_
  } else NA_real_
  p_time_A <- if (!is.null(stab)) {
    r <- grep("s\\(decimal_date\\).*Autumn", rownames(stab))
    if (length(r)) stab[r[1], ncol(stab)] else NA_real_
  } else NA_real_

  edf_site <- if (!is.null(stab)) {
    r <- grep("s\\(SITE_F\\)", rownames(stab), fixed = TRUE)
    if (length(r)) stab[r[1], 1] else NA_real_
  } else NA_real_

  # Helpful anchor on the response scale (0..1): mean fitted detection probability
  mean_p <- tryCatch(mean(fitted(mod, type = "response"), na.rm = TRUE),
                     error = function(e) NA_real_)

  # Assemble a clean, readable row
  tibble(
    `Family`                                  = fam,
    `Samples (n)`                             = n,
    `Mean fitted detection probability`       = round(mean_p, 3),
    `Deviance explained (%)`                  = round(100 * (s$dev.expl %||% NA_real_), 1),
    `Adjusted R²`                             = round(s$r.sq %||% NA_real_, 3),
    `AIC`                                     = round(aic, 1),
    `Season effect: Autumn vs Spring (Estimate)` = round(est_season, 3),
    `Season effect SE`                        = round(se_season, 3),
    `Season effect p-value`                   = fmt_p(p_season),
    `Time smooth edf — Spring`                = round(edf_time_S, 2),
    `Time smooth p — Spring`                  = fmt_p(p_time_S),
    `Time smooth edf — Autumn`                = round(edf_time_A, 2),
    `Time smooth p — Autumn`                  = fmt_p(p_time_A),
    `Site-to-site heterogeneity (edf)`        = round(edf_site, 1)
  )
}

# ----------------- iterate across families & print ----------------

# Only keep families that actually have a fitted model object
fam_names <- names(family_models_binom)
fam_names <- fam_names[!vapply(family_models_binom, is.null, logical(1))]

# Build the table (one row per family) and drop any all-NA columns defensively
binom_summary_table <- purrr::map2_dfr(fam_names, family_models_binom[fam_names], one_binom_row) %>%
  dplyr::select(where(~ any(!is.na(.))))

# Display in console
print(binom_summary_table)

cat("\nNotes:\n",
    "- 'edf' = effective degrees of freedom of the smooth (higher = more wiggle).\n",
    "- Season effect compares Autumn to Spring on the logit scale (binomial link).\n",
    "- 'Deviance explained' and 'Adjusted R²' are mgcv metrics for model fit; R² is a pseudo-R² for binomial.\n",
    "- Mean fitted detection probability is averaged over all samples used in the model.\n", sep = "")

```
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# ORDERED CATEGORICAL (ocat) GAM — ONE FAMILY (with Spring/Autumn)
# + Calibration from later numeric data (per-class medians)
# + Plots: class-counts (with %), by-season counts, partial smooths,
#          faceted smooths (gratia), calibrated expected abundance,
#          P(detection) over time (if available), and diagnostics
#
# RATIONALE
# • Model: mgcv::bam with family = ocat(R = 6) for ordered categories
#   representing abundance classes: 0, 1, 2–9, 10–99, 100–999, 1000+.
# • Seasonality: Spring (Mar–May) vs Autumn (Sep–Nov) 
# • Random site effect: s(SITE_F, bs = "re") to account for spatial heterogeneity.
# • “Calibration”: use later numeric rows to estimate a representative abundance 
#   for each class and season, then convert the class-probability predictions
#   into expected abundance over time.
# • Outputs: publication-ready figures and gam.check diagnostics.
# ================================================================

# ---- Required packages (safe to re-run) -------------------------------------
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(gratia)
library(mgcv)

devAskNewPage(FALSE)  # do not prompt between plots (batch plotting friendly)

# -------- pick your family here ---------------------------------------------
FAM <- "Aphelocheiridae"   # change as needed; must exist in data_by_family

# Guardrails: ensure the preprocessed list is present and contains the family
stopifnot(exists("data_by_family"))
stopifnot(FAM %in% names(data_by_family))

# -------- helpers ------------------------------------------------------------
# prep_family_df():
# • Ensures TOTAL_NUMBER exists (falls back to TOTAL_RND if needed).
# • Adds SITE_F factor and decimal_date if missing.
# • Derives a 2-level season factor: Spring (3–5) and Autumn (9–11).
# • Filters out rows lacking required fields.
prep_family_df <- function(df) {
  if (!("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) && "TOTAL_RND" %in% names(df)) {
    df$TOTAL_NUMBER <- df$TOTAL_RND
  }
  if (!"SITE_F" %in% names(df)) df$SITE_F <- factor(df$SITE_ID)
  if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
      df$decimal_date <- lubridate::decimal_date(as.Date(df$SAMPLE_DATE))
    } else stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE.")
  }
  # month for season derivation (Spring vs Autumn only, as per EA sampling pattern)
  m <- if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) lubridate::month(df$SAMPLE_DATE)
       else lubridate::month(lubridate::date_decimal(df$decimal_date))
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(season = dplyr::case_when(
             m %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
             m %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
             TRUE        ~ NA_character_
           )) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(season)) %>%                          # keep target seasons only
    dplyr::mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring","Autumn"))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(decimal_date), !is.na(SITE_F))
}

# build_cat6():
# • Constructs the ordered response with 6 classes, using TOTAL_NUMBER if
#   available (preferred). If not, falls back to (lower, upper) bounds.
# • Returns df with:
#     - cat6: ordered factor with levels 0, 1, 2–9, 10–99, 100–999, 1000+
#     - Y   : integer codes 1..6, as required by mgcv::ocat(R = 6)
build_cat6 <- function(df) {
  if ("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) {
    x <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
    cat6 <- dplyr::case_when(
      x <= 0                         ~ "0",
      x == 1                         ~ "1",
      x >= 2   & x <= 9              ~ "2-9",
      x >= 10  & x <= 99             ~ "10-99",
      x >= 100 & x <= 999            ~ "100-999",
      x >= 1000                      ~ "1000+",
      TRUE                           ~ NA_character_
    )
  } else if (all(c("lower","upper") %in% names(df))) {
    # fallback to bounds if exact TOTAL_NUMBER is unavailable
    cat6 <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$upper == 0                 ~ "0",
      df$lower == 1 & df$upper == 1 ~ "1",
      df$upper <= 9                 ~ "2-9",
      df$upper <= 99                ~ "10-99",
      df$upper <= 999               ~ "100-999",
      TRUE                          ~ "1000+"
    )
  } else stop("Need TOTAL_NUMBER or (lower, upper) to define ordered categories.")

  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      cat6 = factor(cat6,
                    levels = c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+"),
                    ordered = TRUE),
      Y    = as.integer(cat6)  # mgcv::ocat uses integer classes 1..R
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Y))
}

# compute_calibration():
# • Implements the “later data calibration” guidance:
#   use later *numeric* observations (e.g., post-2000) to estimate a
#   representative abundance (median) for each ordered class and season.
# • Output: named list season -> named numeric vector of medians per class.
#   These medians convert predicted class probabilities into an expected
#   abundance curve on the original scale.
compute_calibration <- function(df_cat) {
  is_num   <- if ("abundance_type" %in% names(df_cat)) df_cat$abundance_type == "numerical" else TRUE
  has_date <- "SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df_cat)
  post2000 <- if (has_date) lubridate::year(df_cat$SAMPLE_DATE) >= 2000 else TRUE
  ok <- is_num & post2000 & is.finite(df_cat$TOTAL_NUMBER)

  # median per (season, class)
  calib <- df_cat %>%
    dplyr::filter(ok) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(season, cat6) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(median = median(TOTAL_NUMBER, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  # pooled medians per class (used as fallback where season*class is sparse)
  pooled <- df_cat %>%
    dplyr::filter(ok) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cat6) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(med_all = median(TOTAL_NUMBER, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  # choose the season-specific median if available, otherwise the pooled one
  calib <- calib %>%
    dplyr::left_join(pooled, by = "cat6") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(med_use = ifelse(is.finite(median), median, med_all)) %>%
    dplyr::select(season, cat6, med_use)  # <-- explicitly use dplyr::select to avoid masking

  # final: a list per season, with a complete set of class medians (fallbacks if needed)
  out <- lapply(levels(df_cat$season), function(sea) {
    sub <- calib %>% dplyr::filter(season == sea)
    v <- sub$med_use; names(v) <- as.character(sub$cat6)
    all_levels <- c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+")
    if (!all(all_levels %in% names(v))) {
      # conservative defaults if some classes are absent in later numeric data
      fallback <- c("0"=0,"1"=1,"2-9"=5,"10-99"=33,"100-999"=333,"1000+"=2000)
      full <- fallback; full[names(v)] <- v; v <- full
    }
    v
  })
  names(out) <- levels(df_cat$season)
  out
}

# fit_ocat_season():
# • Fits the ordered categorical GAM with season as a main effect and
#   separate smooths of time by season; plus site random effect.
# • Requires at least 3 distinct categories to be estimable.
fit_ocat_season <- function(df_ord) {
  if (length(unique(df_ord$Y)) < 3) stop("Too few ordinal categories to fit ocat.")
  mgcv::bam(
    Y ~ season + s(decimal_date, by = season, k = 10) + s(SITE_F, bs = "re"),
    family   = ocat(R = 6),
    data     = df_ord,
    method   = "fREML",
    discrete = TRUE
  )
}

# get_term_col():
# • Convenience to identify the correct column in predict(..., type="terms")
get_term_col <- function(term_fit, nm) {
  j <- grep(nm, colnames(term_fit), fixed = TRUE)
  if (length(j) != 1L) stop("Couldn't find term column: ", nm)
  j
}

# -------- plots ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Class composition plots:
#    (a) Overall class counts with % labels
#    (b) Class counts by season (dodged bars)
plot_ordinal_class_counts <- function(df_ord, fam, pct_digits = 1) {
  tab <- df_ord %>%
    dplyr::count(cat6, .drop = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n),
                  pct_lab = paste0(formatC(pct, format = "f", digits = pct_digits), "%"))
  p1 <- ggplot(tab, aes(x = cat6, y = n)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue1") +
    geom_text(aes(label = pct_lab), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.6) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .12))) +
    labs(title = paste(fam, "— Ordinal class counts (overall)"),
         x = "Ordinal class", y = "Number of samples") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  print(p1)

  p2 <- ggplot(df_ord, aes(x = cat6, fill = season)) +
    geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(Spring = "#1b9e77", Autumn = "#d95f02"),
                      name = "Season") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .05))) +
    labs(title = paste(fam, "— Ordinal class counts by season"),
         x = "Ordinal class", y = "Number of samples") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  print(p2)
}

# 2) Partial time trend (latent ocat scale) by season:
#    plots s(decimal_date) for Spring and Autumn, with 95% ribbon
plot_ocat_time_partial <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- data.frame(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  # Spring
  newS <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jS   <- get_term_col(prS$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring")
  Sx   <- newS$decimal_date; Sfit <- prS$fit[, jS]; Sse <- prS$se.fit[, jS]
  # Autumn
  newA <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jA   <- get_term_col(prA$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn")
  Ax   <- newA$decimal_date; Afit <- prA$fit[, jA]; Ase <- prA$se.fit[, jA]

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)
  ymin <- min(Sfit - 2*Sse, Afit - 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)
  ymax <- max(Sfit + 2*Sse, Afit + 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(Sx, Sfit, type = "n", ylim = c(ymin, ymax),
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Partial effect (latent ocat scale)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Ordinal time smooth by season"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  polygon(c(Sx, rev(Sx)), c(Sfit - 2*Sse, rev(Sfit + 2*Sse)), col = ciS, border = NA)
  polygon(c(Ax, rev(Ax)), c(Afit - 2*Ase, rev(Afit + 2*Ase)), col = ciA, border = NA)
  lines(Sx, Sfit, lwd = 2.4, col = colS); lines(Ax, Afit, lwd = 2.4, col = colA)
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# 3) Side-by-side season smooths using gratia::draw (for manuscript-ready panels)
plot_faceted_by_season_ocat <- function(mod, fam) {
  sm  <- gratia::smooths(mod)
  sel <- grep("^s\\(decimal_date\\):season", sm, value = TRUE)
  if (length(sel) == 0) sel <- grep("decimal_date", sm, value = TRUE)
  p <- gratia::draw(mod, select = sel, scales = "free", ncol = 2, rug = TRUE)
  p <- p & labs(title = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season"),
                x = "decimal_date", y = "Partial effect")
  print(p)
}

# 4) Calibrated expected abundance over time:
#    • Convert predicted class probabilities into E[count] using the
#      per-class medians derived from later numeric data (compute_calibration).
plot_calibrated_expected_abundance <- function(mod, df_ord, calib_list, fam) {
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df_ord$decimal_date), max(df_ord$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df_ord$season),
    SITE_F       = df_ord$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "response", exclude = "s(SITE_F)")

  # inner: compute expected abundance for a season’s rows, given class medians
  calc_curve <- function(subgrid, probs_or_vec, calib_vec) {
    if (is.matrix(probs_or_vec)) {
      # typical case: matrix of class probabilities (n x 6)
      calib_order <- c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+")
      w <- calib_vec[calib_order]
      exp_abund <- as.numeric(probs_or_vec %*% matrix(w, ncol = 1))
    } else {
      # fallback: vector of expected indices → nearest class median
      idx <- pmin(pmax(round(probs_or_vec), 1), 6)
      calib_order <- c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+")
      w <- calib_vec[calib_order]
      exp_abund <- w[idx]
      message("ocat predict(type='response') was not a probability matrix; used nearest-class medians.")
    }
    data.frame(decimal_date = subgrid$decimal_date, exp_abund = exp_abund)
  }

  # build curves separately for each season
  out_list <- lapply(levels(df_ord$season), function(sea) {
    ii <- grid_t$season == sea
    calc <- if (is.matrix(pr)) pr[ii, , drop = FALSE] else pr[ii]
    curve <- calc_curve(grid_t[ii, , drop = FALSE], calc, calib_list[[sea]])
    curve$season <- sea
    curve
  })
  pd <- do.call(rbind, out_list)

  # plot curves
  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(range(pd$decimal_date), range(pd$exp_abund, na.rm = TRUE), type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Calibrated expected abundance",
       main = paste(fam, "— Calibrated expected abundance over time"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (sea in levels(df_ord$season)) {
    sub <- subset(pd, season == sea)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$exp_abund,
          lwd = 2.4, col = if (sea == "Spring") colS else colA)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# 5) P(detection) over time:
#    • Defined here as 1 - P(class == "0"); only available if predict()
#      returns a full probability matrix. Skips otherwise.
plot_prob_detection_over_time_ocat <- function(mod, df_ord, fam) {
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df_ord$decimal_date), max(df_ord$decimal_date), 
                       length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df_ord$season),
    SITE_F       = df_ord$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "response", exclude = "s(SITE_F)")

  if (!is.matrix(pr) || ncol(pr) < 2) {
    message("ocat predict(type='response') did not return class probabilities; ",
            "cannot compute P(detection) precisely — skipping this plot.")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  grid_t$p_det <- 1 - pr[, 1]  # assumes column 1 corresponds to class "0"

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(range(df_ord$decimal_date), range(grid_t$p_det, na.rm = TRUE), type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Predicted detection probability",
       main = paste(fam, "— Predicted detection probability over time (ocat)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (lev in levels(df_ord$season)) {
    sub <- subset(grid_t, season == lev)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$p_det,
          lwd = 2.4, col = if (lev == "Spring") colS else colA)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# Diagnostics 
plot_qq <- function(mod, fam) {
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  mgcv::qq.gam(mod, rep = 0, pch = 16, cex = 0.45) 
  abline(0, 1, col = "firebrick", lty = 2)
  title(main = paste(fam, "— QQ plot (deviance residuals)"))
  box()
}
plot_resid_vs_fitted <- function(mod, fam) {
  # for ocat, fitted(type="response") is the expected category index (1..6)
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  fit <- fitted(mod,  type = "response")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted category (expected index)", ylab = "Deviance residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Fitted (ocat)"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3)
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_link <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  lp  <- predict(mod, type = "link")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(lp, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Linear predictor (latent ocat scale)", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Linear predictor"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3)
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_hist_resid <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  hist(res, breaks = 40, col = "olivedrab2", border = "olivedrab",
       main = paste(fam, "— Histogram of residuals"),
       xlab = "Deviance residuals")
  box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted <- function(mod, df_ord, fam) {
  # expected index vs observed class (with jitter for visibility)
  fit <- fitted(mod, type = "response")
  y   <- df_ord$Y
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, jitter(y, amount = 0.06), pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted category (expected index)", ylab = "Observed class (jittered 1–6)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted (ocat)"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}

# ------------------ gam.check helper -------------------------------
# • Produces the usual gam.check textual diagnostics (k-index, EDF checks)
#   while suppressing the default diagnostic plots (to keep your graphics
#   device uncluttered). The numbers still appear in the console.
gam_check <- function(model) {
  tmpfile <- tempfile(fileext = if (capabilities("cairo")) ".png" else ".pdf")
  if (capabilities("cairo")) png(tmpfile) else pdf(tmpfile)
  on.exit(try(dev.off(), silent = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  out <- capture.output(mgcv::gam.check(model))
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\nGAM diagnostics (text only)\n",
      strrep("=", 70), "\n", paste(out, collapse = "\n"), "\n", sep = "")
}

# ================== RUN FOR ONE FAMILY =======================================
# 1) Prepare data: adds season/decimal_date/site factor, keeps only Spring & Autumn
df0    <- data_by_family[[FAM]] %>% prep_family_df()

# 2) Build ordered response (Y = 1..6) using TOTAL_NUMBER (preferred) or bounds
df_ord <- build_cat6(df0)

# 3) Descriptive plots of class composition (overall and by season, with % labels)
plot_ordinal_class_counts(df_ord, FAM, pct_digits = 1)

# 4) Calibration: per-class medians from *later numeric data* 
calib <- compute_calibration(df_ord)

# 5) Fit the ordered categorical model with season and site RE
mod <- fit_ocat_season(df_ord)

# 6) Print a full mgcv summary (coefficients, smooths, EDF, R^2, dev.expl.)
cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n",
    "Ordinal (ocat) GAM summary — ", FAM, "\n",
    strrep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
print(summary(mod))

# 7) gam.check: effective degrees of freedom checks, k-index, etc.
gam_check(mod)

# 8) Plots (separate figures; manuscript-ready)
plot_ocat_time_partial(mod, df_ord, FAM)    # partial effect by season (latent scale)
plot_faceted_by_season_ocat(mod, FAM)       # side-by-side smooths via gratia
# expected abundance over time (calibrated)
plot_calibrated_expected_abundance(mod, df_ord, calib, FAM)
# P(detection) = 1 - P(class==0) if probs available
plot_prob_detection_over_time_ocat(mod, df_ord, FAM) 
plot_qq(mod, FAM)                            # QQ plot of deviance residuals
plot_resid_vs_fitted(mod, FAM)               # residuals vs expected category index
plot_resid_vs_link(mod, FAM)                 # residuals vs latent linear predictor
plot_hist_resid(mod, FAM)                    # residual histogram
plot_response_vs_fitted(mod, df_ord, FAM)    # observed class vs expected index


```
```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# ORDERED CATEGORICAL (ocat) GAM — ALL FAMILIES (with Spring/Autumn)
# Includes: class-count plots with exact % labels, partial effects,
#           side-by-side season panels, calibrated expected abundance,
#           P(detection) over time (if available), and diagnostics.
#
# RATIONALE
# • Response is an ordered 6-level class (0, 1, 2–9, 10–99, 100–999, 1000+).
# • Model: mgcv::bam with family = ocat(R = 6); separate time smooths by season
#   plus a site random effect to account for spatial heterogeneity.
# • Seasonality: Spring (Mar–May) and Autumn (Sep–Nov) only, reflecting EA practice.
# • “Calibration”: later numeric observations are used to estimate a representative
#   abundance (median) per class & season. Predicted class probabilities are then
#   converted to calibrated expected abundance.
# • Outputs: reproducible, labelled plots and diagnostics.
# ================================================================

# ---- Packages ----
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(mgcv)
library(gratia)
library(patchwork)  # for using `&` to add labs to all panels from gratia::draw

# ------------ families to run -----------------------------------
FAMILIES <- c("Aphelocheiridae","Brachycentridae","Cordulegastridae",
              "Odontoceridae","Potamanthidae")

# Guardrails: make sure the pre-processed per-family list exists and overlaps
stopifnot(exists("data_by_family"))
FAMILIES <- intersect(FAMILIES, names(data_by_family))
stopifnot(length(FAMILIES) > 0)

devAskNewPage(FALSE)  # do not prompt between plots (batch-friendly)

# ------------ helpers -------------------------------------------
# prep_family_df():
# • Ensures TOTAL_NUMBER exists (falls back to TOTAL_RND if needed).
# • Adds SITE_F factor and decimal_date if missing.
# • Derives a 2-level season factor: Spring (3–5) and Autumn (9–11).
# • Filters out rows lacking required fields.
prep_family_df <- function(df) {
  if (!("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) && "TOTAL_RND" %in% names(df)) {
    df$TOTAL_NUMBER <- df$TOTAL_RND
  }
  if (!"SITE_F" %in% names(df)) df$SITE_F <- factor(df$SITE_ID)
  if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
      df$decimal_date <- lubridate::decimal_date(as.Date(df$SAMPLE_DATE))
    } else stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE.")
  }
  # Month for season derivation (Spring vs Autumn only)
  m <- if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
    lubridate::month(df$SAMPLE_DATE)
  } else {
    lubridate::month(lubridate::date_decimal(df$decimal_date))
  }

  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(season = dplyr::case_when(
      m %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
      m %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
      TRUE        ~ NA_character_
    )) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring","Autumn"))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(decimal_date), !is.na(SITE_F))
}

# build_cat6():
# • Constructs a 6-level ordered response from TOTAL_NUMBER (preferred) or bounds:
#   0, 1, 2–9, 10–99, 100–999, 1000+.
# • Returns df with:
#     - cat6: ordered factor
#     - Y   : integer codes 1..6 for mgcv::ocat(R = 6)
build_cat6 <- function(df) {
  if ("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) {
    x <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
    cat6 <- dplyr::case_when(
      x <= 0                         ~ "0",
      x == 1                         ~ "1",
      x >= 2   & x <= 9              ~ "2-9",
      x >= 10  & x <= 99             ~ "10-99",
      x >= 100 & x <= 999            ~ "100-999",
      x >= 1000                      ~ "1000+",
      TRUE                           ~ NA_character_
    )
  } else if (all(c("lower","upper") %in% names(df))) {
    cat6 <- dplyr::case_when(
      df$upper == 0                 ~ "0",
      df$lower == 1 & df$upper == 1 ~ "1",
      df$upper <= 9                 ~ "2-9",
      df$upper <= 99                ~ "10-99",
      df$upper <= 999               ~ "100-999",
      TRUE                          ~ "1000+"
    )
  } else stop("Need TOTAL_NUMBER or (lower, upper) to define ordered categories.")

  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      cat6 = factor(cat6, levels = c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+"), ordered = TRUE),
      Y    = as.integer(cat6)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Y))
}

# compute_calibration():
# • Implements: use later *numeric* rows to get a representative abundance
#   (median) for each class & season. Falls back to pooled medians or
#   conservative defaults where needed.
# • Output: named list season -> named numeric vector (per-class medians).
compute_calibration <- function(df_cat) {
  is_num   <- if ("abundance_type" %in% names(df_cat)) df_cat$abundance_type == "numerical" else TRUE
  has_date <- "SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df_cat)
  post2000 <- if (has_date) lubridate::year(df_cat$SAMPLE_DATE) >= 2000 else TRUE
  ok <- is_num & post2000 & is.finite(df_cat$TOTAL_NUMBER)

  # Season × class medians from later numeric observations
  calib <- df_cat %>%
    dplyr::filter(ok) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(season, cat6) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(median = median(TOTAL_NUMBER, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  # Pooled class medians as fallback
  pooled <- df_cat %>%
    dplyr::filter(ok) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(cat6) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(med_all = median(TOTAL_NUMBER, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  # Prefer season-specific medians; otherwise use pooled values
  calib <- calib %>%
    dplyr::left_join(pooled, by = "cat6") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(med_use = ifelse(is.finite(median), median, med_all)) %>%
    dplyr::select(season, cat6, med_use)   # <-- Fully qualified to avoid masking

  # Convert to list per season with complete class coverage
  out <- lapply(levels(df_cat$season), function(sea) {
    sub <- calib %>% dplyr::filter(season == sea)
    v <- sub$med_use; names(v) <- as.character(sub$cat6)
    all_levels <- c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+")
    if (!all(all_levels %in% names(v))) {
      # Conservative fallback where some classes are absent
      fallback <- c("0"=0,"1"=1,"2-9"=5,"10-99"=33,"100-999"=333,"1000+"=2000)
      full <- fallback; full[names(v)] <- v; v <- full
    }
    v
  })
  names(out) <- levels(df_cat$season)
  out
}

# fit_ocat_season():
# • Ordered categorical GAM with separate time smooths by season + site RE.
# • Requires ≥ 3 distinct ordered categories to fit.
fit_ocat_season <- function(df_ord) {
  if (length(unique(df_ord$Y)) < 3) stop("Too few ordinal categories to fit ocat.")
  mgcv::bam(
    Y ~ season + s(decimal_date, by = season, k = 10) + s(SITE_F, bs = "re"),
    family   = ocat(R = 6),
    data     = df_ord,
    method   = "fREML",
    discrete = TRUE
  )
}

# get_term_col():
# • Helper to locate a term’s column in predict(..., type = "terms").
get_term_col <- function(term_fit, nm) {
  j <- grep(nm, colnames(term_fit), fixed = TRUE)
  if (length(j) != 1L) stop("Couldn't find term column: ", nm)
  j
}

# ---------------- gam.check ----------------
# • Captures gam.check() textual diagnostics (EDF checks, k-index) 
gam_check <- function(model) {
  tmpfile <- tempfile(fileext = if (capabilities("cairo")) ".png" else ".pdf")
  if (capabilities("cairo")) png(tmpfile) else pdf(tmpfile)
  on.exit(try(dev.off(), silent = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  out <- capture.output(mgcv::gam.check(model))
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\nGAM diagnostics (text only)\n",
      strrep("=", 70), "\n", paste(out, collapse = "\n"), "\n", sep = "")
}

# ------------ plots ----------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Class composition: (a) overall with exact % labels; (b) by season
plot_ordinal_class_counts <- function(df_ord, fam, pct_digits = 1) {
  # Overall counts + % labels
  tab <- df_ord %>%
    dplyr::count(cat6, .drop = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n),
                  pct_lab = paste0(formatC(pct, format = "f", digits = pct_digits), "%"))
  p1 <- ggplot(tab, aes(x = cat6, y = n)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue1") +
    geom_text(aes(label = pct_lab), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.6) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .12))) +
    labs(title = paste(fam, "— Ordinal class counts (overall)"),
         x = "Ordinal class", y = "Number of samples") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  print(p1)

  # By-season counts
  p2 <- ggplot(df_ord, aes(x = cat6, fill = season)) +
    geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(Spring = "#1b9e77", Autumn = "#d95f02"),
                      name = "Season") +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, .05))) +
    labs(title = paste(fam, "— Ordinal class counts by season"),
         x = "Ordinal class", y = "Number of samples") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
  print(p2)
}

# 2) Partial time trend on latent ocat scale (by season) with 95% ribbons
plot_ocat_time_partial <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- data.frame(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  # Spring
  newS <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jS   <- get_term_col(prS$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring")
  Sx   <- newS$decimal_date; Sfit <- prS$fit[, jS]; Sse <- prS$se.fit[, jS]

  # Autumn
  newA <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jA   <- get_term_col(prA$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn")
  Ax   <- newA$decimal_date; Afit <- prA$fit[, jA]; Ase <- prA$se.fit[, jA]

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)
  ymin <- min(Sfit - 2*Sse, Afit - 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)
  ymax <- max(Sfit + 2*Sse, Afit + 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(Sx, Sfit, type = "n", ylim = c(ymin, ymax),
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Partial effect (latent ocat scale)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Ordinal time smooth by season"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  polygon(c(Sx, rev(Sx)), c(Sfit - 2*Sse, rev(Sfit + 2*Sse)), col = ciS, border = NA)
  polygon(c(Ax, rev(Ax)), c(Afit - 2*Ase, rev(Afit + 2*Ase)), col = ciA, border = NA)
  lines(Sx, Sfit, lwd = 2.4, col = colS); lines(Ax, Afit, lwd = 2.4, col = colA)
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# 3) Side-by-side season smooths via gratia::draw (clean panels for reporting)
plot_faceted_by_season_ocat <- function(mod, fam) {
  sm  <- gratia::smooths(mod)
  sel <- grep("^s\\(decimal_date\\):season", sm, value = TRUE)
  if (length(sel) == 0) sel <- grep("decimal_date", sm, value = TRUE)
  p <- gratia::draw(mod, select = sel, scales = "free", ncol = 2, rug = TRUE)
  p <- p & labs(title = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season"),
                x = "decimal_date", y = "Partial effect")
  print(p)
}

# 4) Calibrated expected abundance over time:
#    Convert predicted class probabilities into E[count] using per-class medians.
plot_calibrated_expected_abundance <- function(mod, df_ord, calib_list, fam) {
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df_ord$decimal_date), max(df_ord$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df_ord$season),
    SITE_F       = df_ord$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "response", exclude = "s(SITE_F)")

  calc_curve <- function(subgrid, probs_or_vec, calib_vec) {
    if (is.matrix(probs_or_vec)) {
      # Typical case: matrix of class probabilities (n x 6)
      calib_order <- c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+")
      w <- calib_vec[calib_order]
      exp_abund <- as.numeric(probs_or_vec %*% matrix(w, ncol = 1))
    } else {
      # Fallback: vector of expected indices → nearest class median
      idx <- pmin(pmax(round(probs_or_vec), 1), 6)
      calib_order <- c("0","1","2-9","10-99","100-999","1000+")
      w <- calib_vec[calib_order]
      exp_abund <- w[idx]
      message("ocat predict(type='response') was not a probability matrix; used nearest-class medians.")
    }
    data.frame(decimal_date = subgrid$decimal_date, exp_abund = exp_abund)
  }

  # Build season-specific curves
  out_list <- lapply(levels(df_ord$season), function(sea) {
    ii <- grid_t$season == sea
    calc <- if (is.matrix(pr)) pr[ii, , drop = FALSE] else pr[ii]
    curve <- calc_curve(grid_t[ii, , drop = FALSE], calc, calib_list[[sea]])
    curve$season <- sea
    curve
  })
  pd <- do.call(rbind, out_list)

  # Plot curves
  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(range(pd$decimal_date), range(pd$exp_abund, na.rm = TRUE), type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Calibrated expected abundance",
       main = paste(fam, "— Calibrated expected abundance over time"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (sea in levels(df_ord$season)) {
    sub <- subset(pd, season == sea)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$exp_abund,
          lwd = 2.4, col = if (sea == "Spring") colS else colA)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# 5) P(detection) over time (optional): 1 - P(class == "0") if class probs available
plot_prob_detection_over_time_ocat <- function(mod, df_ord, fam) {
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df_ord$decimal_date), max(df_ord$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df_ord$season),
    SITE_F       = df_ord$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "response", exclude = "s(SITE_F)")

  if (!is.matrix(pr) || ncol(pr) < 2) {
    message("ocat predict(type='response') did not return class probabilities; ",
            "cannot compute P(detection) precisely — skipping this plot.")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  grid_t$p_det <- 1 - pr[, 1]  # assume column 1 is class "0"

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(range(df_ord$decimal_date), range(grid_t$p_det, na.rm = TRUE), type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Predicted detection probability",
       main = paste(fam, "— Predicted detection probability over time (ocat)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (lev in levels(df_ord$season)) {
    sub <- subset(grid_t, season == lev)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$p_det,
          lwd = 2.4, col = if (lev == "Spring") colS else colA)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# Diagnostics (standardised look across models)
plot_qq <- function(mod, fam) {
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  mgcv::qq.gam(mod, rep = 0, pch = 16, cex = 0.45)
  abline(0, 1, col = "firebrick", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  title(main = paste(fam, "— QQ plot (deviance residuals)"))
  box()
}
plot_resid_vs_fitted <- function(mod, fam) {
  # For ocat, fitted(type="response") returns expected category index (1..6)
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  fit <- fitted(mod,  type = "response")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted category (expected index)", ylab = "Deviance residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Fitted (ocat)"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3)
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_link <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  lp  <- predict(mod, type = "link")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(lp, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Linear predictor (latent ocat scale)", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Linear predictor"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3)
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_hist_resid <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  hist(res, breaks = 40, col = "olivedrab2", border = "olivedrab",
       main = paste(fam, "— Histogram of residuals"),
       xlab = "Deviance residuals")
  box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted <- function(mod, df_ord, fam) {
  fit <- fitted(mod, type = "response")
  y   <- df_ord$Y
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, jitter(y, amount = 0.06), pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted category (expected index)", ylab = "Observed class (jittered 1–6)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted (ocat)"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}

# ------------ run over all families -----------------------------------------
results_ocat <- setNames(vector("list", length(FAMILIES)), FAMILIES)

for (fam in FAMILIES) {
  message("\n=== Processing ", fam, " ===")

  # 1) Prep data (add season/SITE_F/decimal_date; keep only Spring & Autumn)
  df0 <- try(prep_family_df(data_by_family[[fam]]), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(df0, "try-error") || !nrow(df0)) { message("  No usable data."); next }

  # 2) Build ordered response (Y = 1..6) from TOTAL_NUMBER (or bounds)
  df_ord <- try(build_cat6(df0), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(df_ord, "try-error") || !nrow(df_ord)) { message("  Could not build ordinal response."); next }

  # 0) Descriptive class composition (overall + by season)
  plot_ordinal_class_counts(df_ord, fam, pct_digits = 1)

  # 3) Calibration medians from later numeric data (season × class)
  calib <- compute_calibration(df_ord)

  # 4) Fit ordered categorical GAM with season and site RE
  mod <- try(fit_ocat_season(df_ord), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(mod, "try-error")) { message("  Model failed."); next }
  results_ocat[[fam]] <- list(data = df_ord, calib = calib, model = mod)

  # 5) Time effect on latent scale (by season)
  plot_ocat_time_partial(mod, df_ord, fam)

  # 6) Side-by-side smooths (tidy reporting)
  plot_faceted_by_season_ocat(mod, fam)

  # 7) Calibrated expected abundance over time (response-scale interpretability)
  plot_calibrated_expected_abundance(mod, df_ord, calib, fam)

  # 8) P(detection) over time (if class probabilities are returned)
  plot_prob_detection_over_time_ocat(mod, df_ord, fam)

  # 9) Diagnostics (QQ, residual plots, histogram, response vs fitted)
  plot_qq(mod, fam)
  plot_resid_vs_fitted(mod, fam)
  plot_resid_vs_link(mod, fam)
  plot_hist_resid(mod, fam)
  plot_response_vs_fitted(mod, df_ord, fam)

  # 10) Full mgcv summary 
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\n",
      "Ordinal (ocat) GAM summary — ", fam, "\n",
      strrep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
  print(summary(mod))

  # 11) gam.check (k-index, EDF adequacy) 
  gam_check(mod)
}

```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# OCAT (Ordered Categorical) — COMPACT SUMMARY TABLE (All Families)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# What this does
#   • Assumes you already have `results_ocat` — a named list with one entry per
#     family, where each entry is either:
#       - a fitted mgcv::gam object, or
#       - a list that contains $model (the fitted gam)
#   • Extracts from mgcv::summary() the core, interpretable stats per family:
#       - Sample size (n)
#       - Autumn vs Spring parametric season contrast (estimate & p-value)
#       - Time smooth edf & p-value (Spring and Autumn)
#       - Site random effect edf
#       - Deviance explained (%)
#       - Adjusted R^2 (will often be NA for non-Gaussian links; that’s ok)
#   • Returns a client-friendly table.
#
# Requirements
#   • `results_ocat` must exist in the workspace.
#   • Each fitted model should have seasonal smooths named like:
#       s(decimal_date):seasonSpring and s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn
#   • Base + tidyverse (dplyr, tibble, stringr).
# =============================================================================

# ---- Safety check: do we have the results list? -----------------------------
stopifnot(exists("results_ocat"))

# ---- Small utility helpers (kept lightweight) ----------------

# Safely coerce to numeric; returns NA on failure without noisy warnings.
num  <- function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))

# Round numeric if available; return NA if not.
rnum <- function(x, digits = NULL) {
  y <- num(x)
  if (length(y) == 0 || all(is.na(y))) return(NA_real_)
  if (is.null(digits)) y else round(y, digits)
}

# Significance stars for quick scanning (not used in the final client table,
# but kept here for your internal view if needed later).
star <- function(p) {
  ifelse(is.na(p), "",
         ifelse(p < 0.001, "***",
                ifelse(p < 0.01,  "**",
                       ifelse(p < 0.05, "*",
                              ifelse(p < 0.1,  ".", "")))))
}

# Locate the p-value column name in mgcv's parametric table; be tolerant to variants.
get_p_col <- function(ptab) {
  if (is.null(ptab)) return(NA_character_)
  # Typical names are "Pr(>|z|)" or "Pr(>|t|)"
  cand <- grep("^Pr\\(>\\|[tz]\\|\\)$", colnames(ptab), value = TRUE)
  if (length(cand)) return(cand[1])
  # Fallback: any column starting with "Pr("
  cand <- grep("^Pr\\(", colnames(ptab), value = TRUE)
  if (length(cand)) return(cand[1])
  NA_character_
}

# Pull a parametric term's (estimate, p) from mgcv summary's p.table by rowname.
# Tries exact match (e.g., "seasonAutumn"), then a safe regex-anchored match.
pull_param <- function(ptab, term) {
  if (is.null(ptab) || nrow(ptab) == 0) return(c(NA_real_, NA_real_))
  rn  <- rownames(ptab)
  idx <- which(rn == term)
  if (!length(idx)) {
    pat <- paste0("^", gsub("([\\W])", "\\\\\\1", term), "$")
    idx <- which(grepl(pat, rn))
  }
  if (!length(idx)) return(c(NA_real_, NA_real_))

  est  <- num(ptab[idx[1], "Estimate"])
  pcol <- get_p_col(ptab)

  # Primary path: use the table's p column if present.
  pval <- if (!is.na(pcol) && pcol %in% colnames(ptab)) {
    num(ptab[idx[1], pcol])
  } else {
    # Fallback: derive a two-sided p from a z or t statistic if available.
    stat_col <- intersect(colnames(ptab), c("z value", "t value"))
    if (length(stat_col)) 2 * pnorm(abs(num(ptab[idx[1], stat_col[1]])), lower.tail = FALSE)
    else NA_real_
  }
  c(est, pval)
}

# Pull one smooth row (edf & p-value) from mgcv summary's s.table by pattern.
pull_smooth <- function(st, pattern) {
  if (is.null(st) || nrow(st) == 0) return(list(edf = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
  rn  <- rownames(st)
  hit <- which(stringr::str_detect(rn, stringr::fixed(pattern)))
  if (!length(hit)) return(list(edf = NA_real_, p = NA_real_))
  row <- st[hit[1], , drop = FALSE]
  # mgcv::summary s.table has columns "edf" and "p-value" (Chi.sq or F varies)
  list(edf = rnum(row[, "edf"]), p = rnum(row[, "p-value"]))
}

# Summarise one family/model into a single row tibble
summarise_one <- function(fam, entry) {
  # Each entry may be the model itself or a list with $model.
  mod <- if (inherits(entry, "gam")) entry else entry$model
  if (is.null(mod)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      family = fam, n = NA_integer_,
      beta_seasonAutumn = NA_real_, p_seasonAutumn = NA_real_,
      edf_time_Spring = NA_real_, p_time_Spring = NA_real_,
      edf_time_Autumn = NA_real_, p_time_Autumn = NA_real_,
      edf_siteRE = NA_real_, dev_expl = NA_real_, r2_adj = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  s   <- summary(mod)
  st  <- s$s.table
  pt  <- s$p.table

  # Parametric season contrast: Autumn vs Spring on the latent/ocat scale
  pa <- pull_param(pt, "seasonAutumn")

  # Seasonal time smooths and site random-effect edf
  sp <- pull_smooth(st, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring")
  au <- pull_smooth(st, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn")
  re <- pull_smooth(st, "s(SITE_F)")

  tibble::tibble(
    family            = fam,
    n                 = suppressWarnings(tryCatch(nobs(mod), error = function(e) NA_integer_)),
    beta_seasonAutumn = rnum(pa[1], 3),
    p_seasonAutumn    = ifelse(is.na(pa[2]), NA_real_, signif(num(pa[2]), 3)),
    edf_time_Spring   = rnum(sp$edf, 2),
    p_time_Spring     = ifelse(is.na(sp$p), NA_real_, signif(sp$p, 3)),
    edf_time_Autumn   = rnum(au$edf, 2),
    p_time_Autumn     = ifelse(is.na(au$p), NA_real_, signif(au$p, 3)),
    edf_siteRE        = rnum(re$edf, 1),
    dev_expl          = rnum(100 * num(s$dev.expl), 1),  # % deviance explained
    r2_adj            = rnum(num(s$r.sq), 3)             # may be NA for non-Gaussian
  )
}

# ---- Build the raw per-family summary table ----------------------------------

# Ensure we have names; if not, create placeholders to avoid dropping entries.
fam_names <- names(results_ocat)
if (is.null(fam_names) || any(!nzchar(fam_names))) {
  fam_names <- paste0("fam_", seq_along(results_ocat))
  names(results_ocat) <- fam_names
}

# Map family names + entries to tidy rows (base Map avoids extra dependencies).
rows <- Map(summarise_one, fam = fam_names, entry = results_ocat)

# Bind into a single data frame and add significance stars
ocat_summary_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(rows) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    sig_seasonAutumn = star(p_seasonAutumn),
    sig_time_Spring  = star(p_time_Spring),
    sig_time_Autumn  = star(p_time_Autumn)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(family)

# ---- Produce the compact table --------------

# p-value formatter 
fmt_p <- function(x) {
  ifelse(is.na(x), NA_character_,
         ifelse(x < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 3)))
}

ocat_summary_clean <- ocat_summary_table %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Family`                                   = family,
    `Samples (n)`                              = n,
    `Deviance explained (%)`                   = dev_expl,
    `Season effect p-value (Autumn vs Spring)` = fmt_p(p_seasonAutumn),
    `Time trend edf — Spring`                  = edf_time_Spring,
    `Time trend p-value — Spring`              = fmt_p(p_time_Spring),
    `Time trend edf — Autumn`                  = edf_time_Autumn,
    `Time trend p-value — Autumn`              = fmt_p(p_time_Autumn),
    `Site-to-site heterogeneity (edf)`         = edf_siteRE
  ) %>%
  { 
    df <- .
    keep <- names(df)[colSums(!is.na(df)) > 0]   # drop columns that are entirely NA
    dplyr::select(df, dplyr::all_of(keep))
  } %>%
  dplyr::arrange(`Family`)

# ---- Output: print to console ----------------------

print(ocat_summary_clean)

cat("\nNotes:\n",
    "- 'edf' = effective degrees of freedom of a smooth (higher = more wiggle).\n",
    "- Season contrast is Autumn vs Spring on the ordinal latent scale.\n",
    "- 'Deviance explained' is mgcv's fit metric analogous to R^2 for non-Gaussian models.\n")

```


```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# C-POIS (count Poisson) GAM — ONE FAMILY (Spring/Autumn)
# Plots:
#  1) Partial time smooths by season (overlay)
#  2) Partial time smooths by season (side-by-side panels)
#  3) Mean abundance μ(t) over time (on original count scale)
#  4) Detection probability over time, p(t) = 1 - exp(-μ(t))
#  5) QQ (deviance resid)  [no central rectangle]
#  6) Residuals vs fitted mean (μ̂)
#  7) Residuals vs linear predictor (log μ̂)
#  8) Histogram of residuals
#  9) Response (counts) vs fitted mean (μ̂)
#
# RATIONALE
# • We model raw counts (TOTAL_NUMBER) with a Poisson GAM (log link).
# • Time effect is allowed to vary by season (Spring vs Autumn) via
#   separate smooths; SITE_F is included as a random effect (RE) to
#   account for persistent, site-specific differences.
# • We report μ(t) on the original count scale and the implied
#   detection probability p(t) = 1 - exp(-μ(t)) for interpretability.
# • Diagnostics include QQ, residuals vs fitted/link, histogram, and
#   overdispersion checks:
#     - Pearson χ² / df
#     - SD of randomized-quantile residuals (ideal ≈ 1)
#     - “Imputed” Pearson φ via one ZTP draw for zeros
# ================================================================

devAskNewPage(FALSE)  # batch plotting: don't prompt for new graphics pages

# ---- choose the family ----
FAM <- "Aphelocheiridae"   # change as needed

# Basic guards: ensure preprocessed list exists and contains this family
stopifnot(exists("data_by_family"))
stopifnot(FAM %in% names(data_by_family))

# ---------- helpers ----------
# prep_family_df():
# • Ensures TOTAL_NUMBER exists (falls back to TOTAL_RND if raw is absent).
# • Adds SITE_F factor and decimal_date when missing.
# • Derives a 2-level season factor: Spring (Mar–May) and Autumn (Sep–Nov).
# • Filters to rows with valid site, date, and counts (finite).
prep_family_df <- function(df) {
  # Promote a rounded column if raw counts not present
  if (!("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) && "TOTAL_RND" %in% names(df)) {
    df$TOTAL_NUMBER <- df$TOTAL_RND
  }
  if (!"SITE_F" %in% names(df)) df$SITE_F <- factor(df$SITE_ID)
  if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) df$decimal_date <- lubridate::decimal_date(as.Date(df$SAMPLE_DATE))
    else stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE.")
  }
  # Season: Spring (Mar–May), Autumn (Sep–Nov)
  m <- if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) lubridate::month(df$SAMPLE_DATE)
       else lubridate::month(lubridate::date_decimal(df$decimal_date))
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(season = dplyr::case_when(
             m %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
             m %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
             TRUE        ~ NA_character_
           )) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring","Autumn"))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(decimal_date), !is.na(SITE_F), is.finite(TOTAL_NUMBER))
}

# fit_cpois_season():
# • Poisson log-link model with:
#   - season as a parametric factor,
#   - separate smooths of time by season (s(decimal_date, by=season)),
#   - site random effect s(SITE_F, bs="re").
# • k=30 allows moderately flexible long-term trends; discrete=TRUE for speed.
fit_cpois_season <- function(df) {
  mgcv::bam(
    TOTAL_NUMBER ~ season + s(decimal_date, by = season, k = 30) + s(SITE_F, bs = "re"),
    family   = poisson(link = "log"),
    data     = df,
    method   = "fREML",
    discrete = TRUE
  )
}

# get_term_col():
# • Utility to find a column index for a specific smooth in predict(...,
#   type="terms").
get_term_col <- function(term_fit, nm) {
  j <- grep(nm, colnames(term_fit), fixed = TRUE)
  if (length(j) != 1L) stop("Couldn't find term column: ", nm)
  j
}

# ---------- partial smooths ----------
# plot_trend_by_season():
# • Overlaid partial effects of the seasonal time smooths on the log-mean scale.
# • 95% (±2 SE) ribbons are drawn; site RE excluded from predictions.
plot_trend_by_season <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- data.frame(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )

  # Spring
  newS <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jS   <- get_term_col(prS$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring")
  Sx   <- newS$decimal_date; Sfit <- prS$fit[, jS]; Sse <- prS$se.fit[, jS]

  # Autumn
  newA <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jA   <- get_term_col(prA$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn")
  Ax   <- newA$decimal_date; Afit <- prA$fit[, jA]; Ase <- prA$se.fit[, jA]

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)

  ymin <- min(Sfit - 2*Sse, Afit - 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)
  ymax <- max(Sfit + 2*Sse, Afit + 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(Sx, Sfit, type = "n", ylim = c(ymin, ymax),
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Partial effect on log mean(λ)",
       main = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) vs decimal date (by season)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()

  polygon(c(Sx, rev(Sx)), c(Sfit - 2*Sse, rev(Sfit + 2*Sse)), col = ciS, border = NA)
  polygon(c(Ax, rev(Ax)), c(Afit - 2*Ase, rev(Afit + 2*Ase)), col = ciA, border = NA)
  lines(Sx, Sfit, lwd = 2.4, col = colS)
  lines(Ax, Afit, lwd = 2.4, col = colA)
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# plot_faceted_by_season():
# • Side-by-side panels of the two seasonal smooths using gratia::draw()
plot_faceted_by_season <- function(mod, fam) {
  sm  <- gratia::smooths(mod)
  sel <- grep("^s\\(decimal_date\\):season", sm, value = TRUE)
  if (length(sel) == 0) sel <- grep("decimal_date", sm, value = TRUE)
  p <- gratia::draw(mod, select = sel, scales = "free", ncol = 2, rug = TRUE)
  p <- p & labs(title = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season"),
                x = "decimal_date", y = "Partial effect")
  print(p)
}

# ---------- μ(t) and detection probability ----------
# plot_mu_over_time():
# • Predicts μ(t) on the original count scale by applying the inverse
#   log link to the linear predictor; ±2*SE ribbons are shown (approx).
# • s(SITE_F) is excluded so curves represent population-level trends.
plot_mu_over_time <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  # predictions with site RE held fixed: exclude the random-effect term
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df$season),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr  <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  mu  <- exp(pr$fit)                   # inverse log link
  lwr <- exp(pr$fit - 2*pr$se.fit)
  upr <- exp(pr$fit + 2*pr$se.fit)
  grid_t$mu <- mu; grid_t$lwr <- lwr; grid_t$upr <- upr

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  rng <- range(c(lwr, upr), na.rm = TRUE)
  plot(range(df$decimal_date), rng, type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Mean abundance, μ(t)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Predicted mean abundance over time"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (lev in levels(df$season)) {
    sub <- subset(grid_t, season == lev)
    ci  <- if (lev == "Spring") ciS else ciA
    col <- if (lev == "Spring") colS else colA
    polygon(c(sub$decimal_date, rev(sub$decimal_date)),
            c(sub$lwr,          rev(sub$upr)), col = ci, border = NA)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$mu, lwd = 2.4, col = col)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# plot_detection_over_time():
# • For Poisson counts, P(N>0) = 1 - exp(-μ). We transform μ(t) and
#   its ±2*SE envelope to show predicted detection probability curves.
plot_detection_over_time <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  # p(t) = 1 - exp(-μ(t))
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df$season),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr  <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  mu  <- exp(pr$fit)
  mu_l <- exp(pr$fit - 2*pr$se.fit)
  mu_u <- exp(pr$fit + 2*pr$se.fit)

  p   <- 1 - exp(-mu)
  p_l <- 1 - exp(-mu_l)
  p_u <- 1 - exp(-mu_u)

  grid_t$p <- p; grid_t$lwr <- p_l; grid_t$upr <- p_u

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  rng <- range(c(p_l, p_u), na.rm = TRUE)
  plot(range(df$decimal_date), rng, type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Predicted detection probability",
       main = paste(fam, "— Detection probability over time (Poisson)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (lev in levels(df$season)) {
    sub <- subset(grid_t, season == lev)
    ci  <- if (lev == "Spring") ciS else ciA
    col <- if (lev == "Spring") colS else colA
    polygon(c(sub$decimal_date, rev(sub$decimal_date)),
            c(sub$lwr,          rev(sub$upr)), col = ci, border = NA)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$p, lwd = 2.4, col = col)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# ---------- diagnostics ----------
# • Standard residual diagnostic set to assess fit and variance assumptions.
plot_qq <- function(mod, fam) {
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  mgcv::qq.gam(mod, rep = 0, pch = 16, cex = 0.45)
  abline(0, 1, col = "firebrick", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  title(main = paste(fam, "— QQ plot (deviance residuals)"))
  box()
}
plot_resid_vs_fitted <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  fit <- fitted(mod,  type = "response")   # μ̂
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted mean (μ̂)", ylab = "Deviance residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Fitted"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3); grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_link <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  lp  <- predict(mod, type = "link")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(lp, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Linear predictor (log μ̂)", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Linear predictor"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3); grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_hist_resid <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  hist(res, breaks = 40, col = "grey80", border = "white",
       main = paste(fam, "— Histogram of residuals"),
       xlab = "Deviance residuals")
  box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  fit <- fitted(mod, type = "response")   # μ̂
  y   <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, y, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted mean (μ̂)", ylab = "Observed count",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}

# ---------------- gam.check helper ----------------
# • Captures mgcv::gam.check output (EDF adequacy, k-index).
gam_check <- function(model) {
  tmpfile <- tempfile(fileext = if (capabilities("cairo")) ".png" else ".pdf")
  if (capabilities("cairo")) png(tmpfile) else pdf(tmpfile)
  on.exit(try(dev.off(), silent = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  out <- capture.output(mgcv::gam.check(model))
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 68), "\nGAM diagnostics\n",
      strrep("=", 68), "\n", paste(out, collapse = "\n"), "\n", sep = "")
}

# ---------------- extra overdispersion indicators ----------------
# 1) SD of randomized quantile (Dunn–Smyth) residuals for Poisson
rqres_sd_pois <- function(y, mu, seed = 123) {
  lo <- ppois(pmax(y - 1L, -1L), lambda = mu)   # F(y-1); F(-1)=0 for y=0
  hi <- ppois(y,                 lambda = mu)   # F(y)
  set.seed(seed)
  u  <- lo + (hi - lo) * runif(length(y))
  u  <- pmin(pmax(u, 1e-12), 1 - 1e-12)
  r  <- qnorm(u)
  sd(r[is.finite(r)], na.rm = TRUE)
}

# 2) “Imputed” Pearson φ using one draw from zero-truncated Poisson for zeros
imputed_phi_ztpois <- function(y, mu, df_resid, seed = 456) {
  y_imp <- y
  z     <- which(y == 0L)
  if (length(z)) {
    set.seed(seed)
    p0   <- exp(-mu[z])                                      # P(Y=0)
    u    <- runif(length(z), min = p0, max = 1 - 1e-12)      # truncate to (p0,1)
    y_z  <- qpois(u, lambda = mu[z])                         # inverse CDF
    y_z[y_z < 1L] <- 1L                                      # guard
    y_imp[z] <- y_z
  }
  sum((y_imp - mu)^2 / pmax(mu, 1e-12)) / df_resid
}

# ---------- run ----------
# 1) Prepare data for the chosen family (adds season, SITE_F, decimal_date, etc.)
df_raw <- data_by_family[[FAM]]
df     <- prep_family_df(df_raw)

# 2) Fit Poisson GAM with seasonal time smooths + site RE
mod <- fit_cpois_season(df)

# 3) Print model summary and overdispersion metrics
cat("\n", strrep("=", 68), "\nC-Pois GAM summary — ", FAM, "\n", strrep("=", 68), "\n", sep = "")
print(summary(mod))

# Pearson χ² / df
phi <- sum(residuals(mod, type = "pearson")^2) / mod$df.residual
cat(sprintf("\nApprox. overdispersion (Pearson χ² / df): %.2f\n", phi))

# SD of randomized-quantile residuals (ideal ≈ 1)
mu_hat <- fitted(mod, type = "response")
sd_rq  <- rqres_sd_pois(df$TOTAL_NUMBER, mu_hat)
cat(sprintf("SD of randomized-quantile residuals (ideal ≈ 1): %.3f\n", sd_rq))

# Imputed Pearson φ via one ZTP draw for zeros
phi_imp <- imputed_phi_ztpois(df$TOTAL_NUMBER, mu_hat, df_resid = mod$df.residual)
cat(sprintf("Imputed Pearson φ (zeros→1 draw from ZTP):       %.2f\n", phi_imp))

# 4) gam.check
gam_check(mod)

# 5) Plots (all labelled, publication-ready)
plot_trend_by_season(mod, df, FAM)         # 1: seasonal partial smooths (overlay)
plot_faceted_by_season(mod, FAM)           # 2: side-by-side panels (gratia)
plot_mu_over_time(mod, df, FAM)            # 3: mean abundance μ(t) with 95% ribbons
plot_detection_over_time(mod, df, FAM)     # 4: p(t) = 1 - exp(-μ(t)) with 95% ribbons
plot_qq(mod, FAM)                          # 5: QQ of deviance residuals
plot_resid_vs_fitted(mod, FAM)             # 6: residuals vs fitted μ̂
plot_resid_vs_link(mod, FAM)               # 7: residuals vs log μ̂
plot_hist_resid(mod, FAM)                  # 8: residual histogram
plot_response_vs_fitted(mod, df, FAM)      # 9: observed vs fitted μ̂

```


```{r, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# C-NORM (sqrt-Gaussian) GAM — ONE FAMILY (Spring/Autumn)
#   - Fits on y = sqrt(mid), where mid = (lower + upper) / 2
#   - Uses weights ~ 1 / (interval width + 1) so narrower intervals have
#     greater influence (exact counts ⇒ width=0 ⇒ highest weight)
#   - Random effect for SITE_F controls for persistent site differences
#   - Two seasonal smooths (Spring vs Autumn) allow different time trends
#
# Plots produced:
#   1) Partial time smooths by season (overlay, √-scale)
#   2) Mean abundance (p-scale) over time — season-balanced single curve
#   3) Partial time smooths by season (side-by-side panels, √-scale)
#   4) Expected abundance over time (back-transformed; two seasonal curves)
#   5) QQ plot (normal residuals)
#   6) Residuals vs fitted (√-scale)
#   7) Residuals vs linear predictor (√-scale)
#   8) Histogram of residuals
#   9) Response vs fitted (√-scale)
#  10) Response vs fitted (original scale, μ̂ vs observed midpoint)
#
# Notes:
# • We fit a Gaussian model on the √(midpoint) to approximate count-like
#   outcomes when we have (lower, upper) bounds rather than exact counts.
# • Confidence ribbons for back-transformed curves are produced by
#   transforming mean±2*SE on √-scale and squaring; simple and conservative.
# • “Mean abundance (p-scale)” = original abundance units (post back-transform).
# ================================================================

devAskNewPage(FALSE)  # avoid interactive prompts between plots during knitting / scripted runs

# ---- choose family ----
FAM <- "Aphelocheiridae"  # change as needed  # <- focal taxon to model (must exist in data_by_family)

# Guardrails
stopifnot(exists("data_by_family"))              # ensure preprocessing objects are available
stopifnot(FAM %in% names(data_by_family))        # defensive: chosen family present

# ---------- helpers ----------
# prep_family_df_cnorm(df)
# Purpose:
#   - Guarantee availability of (lower, upper) bounds; fall back to exact counts if needed.
#   - Construct SITE_F (site factor) and decimal_date if not already present.
#   - Derive season (Spring = Mar–May, Autumn = Sep–Nov) and drop other months.
#   - Compute midpoint and √-transform for modelling; compute interval width-based weights.
# Returns: a cleaned modelling frame with columns: season, mid, width, y_sqrt, w, SITE_F, decimal_date.
prep_family_df_cnorm <- function(df) {
  if (!all(c("lower","upper") %in% names(df))) {
    if ("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) {
      df$lower <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER; df$upper <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER    # treat exact counts as zero-width bounds
    } else if ("TOTAL_RND" %in% names(df)) {
      df$lower <- df$TOTAL_RND; df$upper <- df$TOTAL_RND          # same logic using rounded numeric column
    } else stop("Need (lower, upper) or TOTAL_NUMBER/TOTAL_RND to derive midpoints.")
  }
  if (!"SITE_F" %in% names(df)) df$SITE_F <- factor(df$SITE_ID)   # random-effect factor for sites
  if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) df$decimal_date <- lubridate::decimal_date(as.Date(df$SAMPLE_DATE))
    else stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE.")                 # enforce one of the two time encodings
  }
  m <- if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) lubridate::month(df$SAMPLE_DATE)
       else lubridate::month(lubridate::date_decimal(df$decimal_date))  # month extraction path if only decimal_date present
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      season = dplyr::case_when(           # restrict analysis to main survey seasons
        m %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
        m %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
        TRUE        ~ NA_character_
      ),
      mid    = pmax((lower + upper)/2, 0),           # midpoint on original scale; guard against negatives
      width  = pmax(upper - lower, 0),               # interval width (precision proxy)
      y_sqrt = sqrt(mid),                            # response variable for Gaussian(identity) on √-scale
      w_raw  = 1/(width + 1),                        # higher weight for narrower intervals; exact counts → 1
      w      = w_raw / mean(w_raw, na.rm = TRUE)     # stabilise weights around ~1
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(season)) %>%                # keep Spring/Autumn only
    dplyr::mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring","Autumn"))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(decimal_date), is.finite(y_sqrt), !is.na(SITE_F))  # final safety filters
}

# fit_cnorm_season(df)
# Model:
#   y_sqrt ~ season + s(decimal_date, by=season, k=10) + s(SITE_F, bs="re")
#   • Gaussian family (identity link) on √(mid).
#   • Seasonal smooths permit different temporal shapes by season.
#   • Site random effect captures persistent site-level heterogeneity.
#   • Weights favour precise (narrow) intervals.
fit_cnorm_season <- function(df) {
  mgcv::bam(
    y_sqrt ~ season + s(decimal_date, by = season, k = 10) + s(SITE_F, bs = "re"),
    family   = gaussian(),
    data     = df,
    weights  = df$w,
    method   = "fREML",                                           # efficient REML estimation
    discrete = TRUE                                               # speed up with discretization for large data
  )
}

# get_term_col(): helper to identify the correct smooth column in predict(..., type="terms")
# Throws if not exactly one match is found, preventing silent mis-selection.
get_term_col <- function(term_fit, nm) {
  j <- grep(nm, colnames(term_fit), fixed = TRUE)
  if (length(j) != 1L) stop("Couldn't find term column: ", nm)
  j
}

# ---------- (1) partial smooths overlay ----------
# Produces season-specific partial effects (√-scale) with 95% ribbons.
# Excludes the site random effect from predictions for population-level curves.
plot_trend_by_season_cnorm <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- data.frame(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]                                    # dummy level; removed by exclude="s(SITE_F)"
  )
  newS <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jS   <- get_term_col(prS$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring")
  Sx   <- newS$decimal_date; Sfit <- prS$fit[, jS]; Sse <- prS$se.fit[, jS]

  newA <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jA   <- get_term_col(prA$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn")
  Ax   <- newA$decimal_date; Afit <- prA$fit[, jA]; Ase <- prA$se.fit[, jA]

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"                             # consistent, colour-blind aware palette
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)  # translucent confidence ribbons

  ymin <- min(Sfit - 2*Sse, Afit - 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)            # 95% band union for y-limits
  ymax <- max(Sfit + 2*Sse, Afit + 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(Sx, Sfit, type = "n", ylim = c(ymin, ymax),
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Partial effect on sqrt(mid)",
       main = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season (√-scale)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  polygon(c(Sx, rev(Sx)), c(Sfit - 2*Sse, rev(Sfit + 2*Sse)), col = ciS, border = NA)
  polygon(c(Ax, rev(Ax)), c(Afit - 2*Ase, rev(Afit + 2*Ase)), col = ciA, border = NA)
  lines(Sx, Sfit, lwd = 2.4, col = colS)
  lines(Ax, Afit, lwd = 2.4, col = colA)
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# ---------- (2) Mean abundance (p-scale) — season-balanced single curve -----------
# Constructs a single interpretable trend by:
#   - Predicting seasonal fits on √-scale (identity link).
#   - Averaging Spring & Autumn fits with equal weights (½, ½) on √-scale.
#   - Converting the balanced fit (and CI) back to original abundance units by squaring.
plot_mean_abundance_balanced_cnorm <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  x_grid <- seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300)
  base   <- data.frame(decimal_date = x_grid, SITE_F = df$SITE_F[1])

  # Predict seasonal fits/se on √-scale, excluding site RE
  newS <- transform(base, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  newA <- transform(base, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")

  # Balanced (equal-weight) combination on √-scale
  fit_bal <- 0.5 * (prS$fit + prA$fit)
  se_bal  <- 0.5 * sqrt(prS$se.fit^2 + prA$se.fit^2)  # assume independence between the two seasonal predictions

  # Back-transform to original scale (clip negatives before squaring)
  fit_bal <- pmax(fit_bal, 0)
  mu      <- fit_bal^2
  lwr     <- pmax(fit_bal - 2*se_bal, 0)^2
  upr     <- pmax(fit_bal + 2*se_bal, 0)^2

  # Plot single curve + CI ribbon
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  yrng <- range(c(lwr, upr), na.rm = TRUE)
  plot(x_grid, mu, type = "n", ylim = yrng,
       xlab = "Year (decimal)", ylab = "Mean abundance (p-scale)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Season-balanced mean abundance over time"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  polygon(c(x_grid, rev(x_grid)), c(lwr, rev(upr)),
          col = adjustcolor("#3B7CFF", 0.20), border = NA)
  lines(x_grid, mu, lwd = 2.6, col = "#3B7CFF")
  mtext("Equal weights: ½·Spring + ½·Autumn on √-scale; CI via ±2·SE (then squared).",
        side = 3, line = 0.2, cex = 0.8)
}

# ---------- (3) partial smooths side-by-side ------------
# Uses gratia::draw() for small multiples (one panel per season) on √-scale.
plot_faceted_by_season_cnorm <- function(mod, fam) {
  sm  <- gratia::smooths(mod)
  sel <- grep("^s\\(decimal_date\\):season", sm, value = TRUE)
  if (length(sel) == 0) sel <- grep("decimal_date", sm, value = TRUE)
  p <- gratia::draw(mod, select = sel, scales = "free", ncol = 2, rug = TRUE)
  p <- p & labs(title = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season (√-scale)"),
                x = "decimal_date", y = "Partial effect on sqrt(mid)")
  print(p)
}

# ---------- (4) expected abundance over time (two seasonal curves) ----------
# Back-transforms each seasonal fit: μ̂ = (fit_√)^2, with ribbons from (fit_√ ± 2·SE)^2.
plot_expected_abundance_over_time <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df$season),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr    <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  fit_s <- pmax(pr$fit, 0)
  se_s  <- pmax(pr$se.fit, 0)
  mu    <- fit_s^2
  lwr   <- pmax(fit_s - 2*se_s, 0)^2
  upr   <- pmax(fit_s + 2*se_s, 0)^2
  grid_t$mu <- mu; grid_t$lwr <- lwr; grid_t$upr <- upr

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  rng <- range(c(lwr, upr), na.rm = TRUE)
  plot(range(df$decimal_date), rng, type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Expected abundance (back-transformed)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Expected abundance over time (√-Gaussian)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (lev in levels(df$season)) {
    sub <- subset(grid_t, season == lev)
    ci  <- if (lev == "Spring") ciS else ciA
    col <- if (lev == "Spring") colS else colA
    polygon(c(sub$decimal_date, rev(sub$decimal_date)),
            c(sub$lwr,          rev(sub$upr)), col = ci, border = NA)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$mu, lwd = 2.4, col = col)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# ---------- diagnostics ----------
# Standard diagnostic set for GAMs on √-scale responses.
plot_qq <- function(mod, fam) {
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  mgcv::qq.gam(mod, rep = 0, pch = 16, cex = 0.45)
  abline(0, 1, col = "firebrick", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  title(main = paste(fam, "— QQ plot (normal residuals)")); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_fitted <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  fit <- fitted(mod,  type = "response")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted (sqrt mid)", ylab = "Deviance residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Fitted"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3); grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_link <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  lp  <- predict(mod, type = "link")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(lp, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Linear predictor (sqrt mid)", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Linear predictor"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3); grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_hist_resid <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  hist(res, breaks = 40, col = "goldenrod1", border = "goldenrod3",
       main = paste(fam, "— Histogram of residuals"),
       xlab = "Deviance residuals"); box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted_sqrt <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  fit <- fitted(mod, type = "response")
  y   <- df$y_sqrt
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, y, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted (sqrt mid)", ylab = "Observed (sqrt mid)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted (sqrt scale)"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted_original <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  fit_s <- fitted(mod, type = "response")
  muhat <- pmax(fit_s, 0)^2                                      # back-transform to original scale
  mid   <- df$mid
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(muhat, mid, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted expected abundance (μ̂)", ylab = "Observed midpoint",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted (original scale)"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}

# ------------------ gam_check ------------------
# Captures textual output from mgcv::gam.check() without emitting plots,
# keeping scripted runs tidy; still prints k-indices and residual checks.
gam_check <- function(model) {
  tmpfile <- tempfile(fileext = if (capabilities("cairo")) ".png" else ".pdf")
```
