```{r, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
  if (capabilities("cairo")) png(tmpfile) else pdf(tmpfile)
  on.exit(try(dev.off(), silent = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  out <- capture.output(mgcv::gam.check(model))
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\nGAM diagnostics (text only)\n",
      strrep("=", 70), "\n", paste(out, collapse = "\n"), "\n", sep = "")
}

# ---------- run ----------
df_raw <- data_by_family[[FAM]]                  # retrieve preprocessed family table
df     <- prep_family_df_cnorm(df_raw)           # build modelling inputs (√-response, weights, season, etc.)

cat("Weights summary (centered ~1):\n"); print(summary(df$w))  # quick QC on precision weights

mod <- fit_cnorm_season(df)               # fit seasonal √-Gaussian GAM with site RE

cat("\n", strrep("=", 68), "\nC-Norm (√-Gaussian) GAM summary — ", FAM,
    "\n", strrep("=", 68), "\n", sep = ""); print(summary(mod))  # standard mgcv summary for reporting

gam_check(mod)                                 # diagnostics (k-index, residual tests)

# Plots (all labelled and publication-ready)
plot_trend_by_season_cnorm(mod, df, FAM)     # 1) seasonal smooths (overlay, √-scale)
plot_mean_abundance_balanced_cnorm(mod, df, FAM)        # 2) mean abundance (p-scale)
plot_faceted_by_season_cnorm(mod, FAM)        # 3) seasonal smooths (facets, √-scale)
plot_expected_abundance_over_time(mod, df, FAM)     # 4) expected abundance by season 
plot_qq(mod, FAM)                                          # 5) QQ (normal residuals)
plot_resid_vs_fitted(mod, FAM)                     # 6) residuals vs fitted (√-scale)
plot_resid_vs_link(mod, FAM)             # 7) residuals vs linear predictor (√-scale)
plot_hist_resid(mod, FAM)                                     # 8) residual histogram
plot_response_vs_fitted_sqrt(mod, df, FAM)          # 9) response vs fitted (√-scale)
plot_response_vs_fitted_original(mod, df, FAM)               # 10) response vs fitted 

```

```{r, fig.width=11, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# C-NORM (sqrt-Gaussian) GAM — ALL FAMILIES (Spring/Autumn)
# Purpose
#   • Fit a √-Gaussian GAM to interval data per family:
#       y = sqrt(mid),  where mid = (lower + upper) / 2
#   • Weight each observation by ~ 1/(width+1), where width = upper - lower
#     so narrower (more precise) intervals have more influence.
#   • Include two seasonal smooths (Spring vs Autumn) and a site random effect.
#
# Outputs (per family; matches the one-family script):
#   1) Partial time smooths by season (overlay, √-scale)
#   2) NEW — Mean abundance (p-scale) over time — season-balanced single curve
#   3) Partial time smooths by season (side-by-side panels)
#   4) Expected abundance over time (back-transformed to original scale) with CI 
#      ribbon
#   5) QQ plot (normal residuals)
#   6) Residuals vs fitted (√-scale)
#   7) Residuals vs linear predictor (√-scale)
#   8) Histogram of residuals
#   9) Response vs fitted (√-scale)
#  10) Response vs fitted (original scale, μ̂ vs observed midpoint)
#
# Assumptions & notes
#   • If (lower, upper) are missing, exact numeric counts (TOTAL_NUMBER or TOTAL_RND)
#     are treated as exact bounds (lower = upper = count).
#   • Random effect s(SITE_F, bs="re") controls for persistent site-level differences.
#   • Back-transformed CIs use (fit ± 2·SE)^2 on the √-scale; simple/conservative.
# ================================================================

# Whether side-by-side smooth panels can be drawn 
HAS_GRATIA <- requireNamespace("gratia", quietly = TRUE)

devAskNewPage(FALSE)  # prevent "new page" prompts when plotting many figures

# ---------- families to run ----------
FAMILIES <- c("Aphelocheiridae","Brachycentridae",
              "Cordulegastridae","Odontoceridae","Potamanthidae")

# Guardrails: ensure preprocessed list exists and contains at least one target family
stopifnot(exists("data_by_family"))
FAMILIES <- intersect(FAMILIES, names(data_by_family))
stopifnot(length(FAMILIES) > 0)

# ---------- helpers ----------
# prep_family_df_cnorm(df)
# • Ensures (lower, upper) exist; if not, falls back to TOTAL_NUMBER/TOTAL_RND (exact).
# • Constructs 'season' (Spring=Mar–May; Autumn=Sep–Nov).
# • Computes:
#     mid    = (lower+upper)/2 (clipped to ≥ 0)
#     width  = upper-lower (clipped to ≥ 0)
#     y_sqrt = sqrt(mid)  → response variable
#     w      = scaled weights ~ 1/(width+1) to prioritise precise intervals
# • Adds SITE_F (factor) and decimal_date if missing; drops invalid rows.
prep_family_df_cnorm <- function(df) {
  # If bounds missing, fall back to exact counts
  if (!all(c("lower","upper") %in% names(df))) {
    if ("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) {
      df$lower <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
      df$upper <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
    } else if ("TOTAL_RND" %in% names(df)) {
      df$lower <- df$TOTAL_RND
      df$upper <- df$TOTAL_RND
    } else {
      stop("Need (lower, upper) or numeric TOTAL_NUMBER/TOTAL_RND.")
    }
  }
  if (!"SITE_F" %in% names(df)) df$SITE_F <- factor(df$SITE_ID)
  if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
      df$decimal_date <- lubridate::decimal_date(as.Date(df$SAMPLE_DATE))
    } else stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE.")
  }

  # Spring (Mar–May), Autumn (Sep–Nov)
  m <- if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) lubridate::month(df$SAMPLE_DATE)
       else lubridate::month(lubridate::date_decimal(df$decimal_date))

  df %>%
    mutate(
      season = dplyr::case_when(
        m %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
        m %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
        TRUE        ~ NA_character_
      ),
      mid    = pmax((lower + upper)/2, 0),          # midpoint, non-negative
      width  = pmax(upper - lower, 0),              # interval width, non-negative
      y_sqrt = sqrt(mid),                           # response on √-scale
      w_raw  = 1/(width + 1),                       # narrower intervals ⇒ higher weight
      w      = w_raw / mean(w_raw, na.rm = TRUE)    # stabilise weights around ~1
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
    mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring","Autumn"))) %>%
    filter(is.finite(decimal_date), is.finite(y_sqrt), !is.na(SITE_F))
}

# fit_cnorm_season(df)
# Model:
#   y_sqrt ~ season + s(decimal_date, by=season, k=10) + s(SITE_F, bs="re")
#   • Gaussian family with identity link on √(midpoint).
#   • Seasonal smooths allow different temporal shapes by season.
#   • Random-effect smooth for SITE_F captures site heterogeneity.
#   • Weights implement precision weighting by interval width.
fit_cnorm_season <- function(df) {
  mgcv::bam(
    y_sqrt ~ season + s(decimal_date, by = season, k = 10) + s(SITE_F, bs = "re"),
    family   = gaussian(),   # identity link on √(mid)
    data     = df,
    weights  = df$w,
    method   = "fREML",
    discrete = TRUE
  )
}

# get_term_col(): utility to find the column index for a given smooth
get_term_col <- function(term_fit, nm) {
  j <- grep(nm, colnames(term_fit), fixed = TRUE)
  if (length(j) != 1L) stop("Couldn't find term column: ", nm)
  j
}

# --- 1) Partial time smooths (overlay) ---
# Overlays Spring and Autumn smooths on √-scale with ±2*SE ribbons.
# Excludes the site random effect so curves reflect the population-level trend.
plot_trend_by_season_cnorm <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- data.frame(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  # Spring
  newS <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jS   <- get_term_col(prS$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring")
  Sx   <- newS$decimal_date; Sfit <- prS$fit[, jS]; Sse <- prS$se.fit[, jS]
  # Autumn
  newA <- transform(grid_t, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "terms", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  jA   <- get_term_col(prA$fit, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn")
  Ax   <- newA$decimal_date; Afit <- prA$fit[, jA]; Ase <- prA$se.fit[, jA]

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)

  ymin <- min(Sfit - 2*Sse, Afit - 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)
  ymax <- max(Sfit + 2*Sse, Afit + 2*Ase, na.rm = TRUE)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1), xaxs = "i")
  plot(Sx, Sfit, type = "n", ylim = c(ymin, ymax),
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Partial effect on sqrt(mid)",
       main = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season (√-scale)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  polygon(c(Sx, rev(Sx)), c(Sfit - 2*Sse, rev(Sfit + 2*Sse)), col = ciS, border = NA)
  polygon(c(Ax, rev(Ax)), c(Afit - 2*Ase, rev(Afit + 2*Ase)), col = ciA, border = NA)
  lines(Sx, Sfit, lwd = 2.4, col = colS)
  lines(Ax, Afit, lwd = 2.4, col = colA)
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# --- 2) Mean abundance (p-scale) — season-balanced single curve ---
# Constructs a single interpretable trajectory by:
#   • predicting Spring & Autumn fits (√-scale, excluding site RE),
#   • averaging them with equal weights (½, ½) on √-scale,
#   • and back-transforming to original abundance units by squaring.
plot_mean_abundance_balanced_cnorm <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  x_grid <- seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300)
  base   <- data.frame(decimal_date = x_grid, SITE_F = df$SITE_F[1])

  # Predict seasonal fits/se on √-scale, excluding site RE
  newS <- transform(base, season = factor("Spring", levels = levels(df$season)))
  newA <- transform(base, season = factor("Autumn", levels = levels(df$season)))
  prS  <- predict(mod, newdata = newS, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  prA  <- predict(mod, newdata = newA, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")

  # Balanced (equal-weight) combination on √-scale
  fit_bal <- 0.5 * (prS$fit + prA$fit)
  se_bal  <- 0.5 * sqrt(prS$se.fit^2 + prA$se.fit^2)  # independence approximation

  # Back-transform to original scale (clip negatives before squaring)
  fit_bal <- pmax(fit_bal, 0)
  mu      <- fit_bal^2
  lwr     <- pmax(fit_bal - 2*se_bal, 0)^2
  upr     <- pmax(fit_bal + 2*se_bal, 0)^2

  # Plot single curve + CI ribbon
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  yrng <- range(c(lwr, upr), na.rm = TRUE)
  plot(x_grid, mu, type = "n", ylim = yrng,
       xlab = "Year (decimal)", ylab = "Mean abundance (p-scale)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Season-balanced mean abundance over time"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  polygon(c(x_grid, rev(x_grid)), c(lwr, rev(upr)),
          col = adjustcolor("#3B7CFF", 0.20), border = NA)
  lines(x_grid, mu, lwd = 2.6, col = "#3B7CFF")
  mtext("Equal weights: ½·Spring + ½·Autumn on √-scale; CI via ±2·SE (then squared).",
        side = 3, line = 0.2, cex = 0.8)
}

# --- 3) Partial time smooths (side-by-side panels) ---
# Uses gratia::draw() for clean faceted panels per season on √-scale.
plot_faceted_by_season_cnorm <- function(mod, fam) {
  if (!HAS_GRATIA) { message("gratia not installed; skipping side-by-side panels."); return(invisible()) }
  sm  <- gratia::smooths(mod)
  sel <- grep("^s\\(decimal_date\\):season", sm, value = TRUE)
  if (length(sel) == 0) sel <- grep("decimal_date", sm, value = TRUE)
  p <- gratia::draw(mod, select = sel, scales = "free", ncol = 2, rug = TRUE)
  p <- p + ggplot2::labs(title = paste(fam, "— s(decimal_date) by season (√-scale)"),
                         x = "decimal_date", y = "Partial effect on sqrt(mid)")
  print(p)
}

# --- 4) Expected abundance over time (original scale, back-transformed) ---
# Predict on √-scale (excluding the site RE), then back-transform: μ̂ = fit^2.
# CI ribbon uses (fit ± 2*SE)^2, truncated at 0 for numerical stability.
plot_expected_abundance_over_time <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  grid_t <- expand.grid(
    decimal_date = seq(min(df$decimal_date), max(df$decimal_date), length.out = 300),
    season       = levels(df$season),
    SITE_F       = df$SITE_F[1]
  )
  pr <- predict(mod, newdata = grid_t, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = "s(SITE_F)")
  fit_s <- pmax(pr$fit, 0)
  se_s  <- pmax(pr$se.fit, 0)
  mu    <- fit_s^2
  lwr   <- pmax(fit_s - 2*se_s, 0)^2
  upr   <- pmax(fit_s + 2*se_s, 0)^2

  grid_t$mu <- mu; grid_t$lwr <- lwr; grid_t$upr <- upr

  colS <- "#1b9e77"; colA <- "#d95f02"
  ciS  <- adjustcolor(colS, 0.20); ciA <- adjustcolor(colA, 0.20)

  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1), xaxs = "i")
  rng <- range(c(lwr, upr), na.rm = TRUE)
  plot(range(df$decimal_date), rng, type = "n",
       xlab = "Decimal date", ylab = "Expected abundance (back-transformed)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Expected abundance over time (√-Gaussian)"))
  grid(col = "grey85", lty = "dotted"); box()
  for (lev in levels(df$season)) {
    sub <- subset(grid_t, season == lev)
    ci  <- if (lev == "Spring") ciS else ciA
    col <- if (lev == "Spring") colS else colA
    polygon(c(sub$decimal_date, rev(sub$decimal_date)),
            c(sub$lwr,          rev(sub$upr)), col = ci, border = NA)
    lines(sub$decimal_date, sub$mu, lwd = 2.4, col = col)
  }
  legend("topleft", c("Spring","Autumn"), col = c(colS, colA), lwd = 2.4, bty = "n")
}

# --- 5) to 10) diagnostics & fitted vs observed ---
# Standard diagnostics to assess residual structure and fit quality.
plot_qq <- function(mod, fam) {
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  mgcv::qq.gam(mod, rep = 0, pch = 16, cex = 0.45)
  abline(0, 1, col = "firebrick", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
  title(main = paste(fam, "— QQ plot (normal residuals)")); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_fitted <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  fit <- fitted(mod,  type = "response") # √-scale
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted (sqrt mid)", ylab = "Deviance residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Fitted"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3); grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_resid_vs_link <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  lp  <- predict(mod, type = "link")     # identity = √-scale
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(lp, res, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Linear predictor (sqrt mid)", ylab = "Residuals",
       main = paste(fam, "— Residuals vs Linear predictor"))
  abline(h = 0, col = "grey70", lty = 3); grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_hist_resid <- function(mod, fam) {
  res <- residuals(mod, type = "deviance")
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  hist(res, breaks = 40, col = "goldenrod1", border = "goldenrod3",
       main = paste(fam, "— Histogram of residuals"),
       xlab = "Deviance residuals"); box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted_sqrt <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  fit <- fitted(mod, type = "response")   # √-scale
  y   <- df$y_sqrt
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(fit, y, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted (sqrt mid)", ylab = "Observed (sqrt mid)",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted (sqrt scale)"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}
plot_response_vs_fitted_original <- function(mod, df, fam) {
  fit_s <- fitted(mod, type = "response")
  muhat <- pmax(fit_s, 0)^2
  mid   <- df$mid
  par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = c(4.2,4.8,3.2,1))
  plot(muhat, mid, pch = 16, cex = 0.35,
       xlab = "Fitted expected abundance (μ̂)", ylab = "Observed midpoint",
       main = paste(fam, "— Response vs Fitted (original scale)"))
  grid(col = "grey90"); box()
}

# ------------------ gam.check helper ------------------
# Captures the textual part of mgcv::gam.check() (e.g., k-index tests).
gam_check <- function(model) {
  tmpfile <- tempfile(fileext = if (capabilities("cairo")) ".png" else ".pdf")
  if (capabilities("cairo")) png(tmpfile) else pdf(tmpfile)
  on.exit(try(dev.off(), silent = TRUE), add = TRUE)
  out <- capture.output(mgcv::gam.check(model))
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 70), "\nGAM diagnostics (text only)\n",
      strrep("=", 70), "\n", paste(out, collapse = "\n"), "\n", sep = "")
}

# ---------- run for all families ----------
# 'cnorm_models' will store the fitted model + the prepared data per family
cnorm_models <- setNames(vector("list", length(FAMILIES)), FAMILIES)

for (fam in FAMILIES) {
  message("Fitting C-Norm (season) for: ", fam)

  # 0) Input presence and shape check
  df_raw <- data_by_family[[fam]]
  if (is.null(df_raw) || !nrow(df_raw)) { message("  No data; skipping."); next }

  # 1) Prepare analysis frame for √-Gaussian model
  df <- try(prep_family_df_cnorm(df_raw), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(df, "try-error") || !nrow(df)) { message("  Could not prep; skipping."); next }

  # Quick sanity on weights (should be centred ~1 after scaling)
  cat("\nWeights summary for", fam, "(centered ~1):\n")
  print(summary(df$w))

  # 2) Fit model with seasonal smooths + site random effect
  mod <- try(fit_cnorm_season(df), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(mod, "try-error")) { message("  Model failed; skipping."); next }
  cnorm_models[[fam]] <- list(model = mod, data = df)

  # 3) Print standard mgcv summary (reproducible technical output)
  cat("\n", strrep("=", 72), "\nC-Norm (√-Gaussian) GAM summary — ", fam,
      "\n", strrep("=", 72), "\n", sep = "")
  print(summary(mod))

  # 4) Add textual diagnostics 
  gam_check(mod)

  # 5) Figures (each draws on the active device)
  plot_trend_by_season_cnorm(mod, df, fam)            # 1) overlay partial effect (√-scale)
  plot_mean_abundance_balanced_cnorm(mod, df, fam)    # 2) season-balanced mean abundance (p-scale)
  plot_faceted_by_season_cnorm(mod, fam)              # 3) side-by-side partials (√-scale)
  plot_expected_abundance_over_time(mod, df, fam)     # 4) expected abundance by season (p-scale)
  plot_qq(mod, fam)                                   # 5) QQ plot
  plot_resid_vs_fitted(mod, fam)                      # 6) Residuals vs fitted (√-scale)
  plot_resid_vs_link(mod, fam)                        # 7) Residuals vs linear predictor (√-scale)
  plot_hist_resid(mod, fam)                           # 8) Residual histogram
  plot_response_vs_fitted_sqrt(mod, df, fam)          # 9) Response vs fitted (√-scale)
  plot_response_vs_fitted_original(mod, df, fam)      # 10) Response vs fitted (original scale)
}

```
```{r}
# ================================================================
# COMPACT SUMMARY TABLE — C-NORM (√-Gaussian) GAMs (ALL FAMILIES)
# Purpose:
#   Build a compact, client-ready table of key diagnostics/metrics
#   for all C-Norm models (one row per family) stored in `cnorm_models`.
#
# Output columns (readable names):
#   • Family
#   • Samples(n)
#   • Adj. R² (√-scale)
#   • Deviance explained (%)
#   • AIC
#   • Scale (σ², √-scale)   — Gaussian residual variance on √-scale
#   • Intercept (√-scale), Intercept p
#   • Autumn vs Spring (√-scale), Autumn vs Spring p
#   • Time smooth edf — Spring, Time smooth p — Spring
#   • Time smooth edf — Autumn, Time smooth p — Autumn
#   • Site RE edf (random-effect smooth s(SITE_F))
#
# Assumptions:
#   • The “C-NORM — ALL FAMILIES” pipeline has been run and produced
#     `cnorm_models` (a list with elements $model and $data per family).
#   • If not present, we attempt a minimal refit using `data_by_family`
#     with the same modelling formula/weights as the pipeline.
# Notes:
#   • Numbers are rounded for readability (p-values with `signif()`,
#     others with `round()`); adjust as required for reporting.
# ================================================================

# ------ Safety net: ensure models exist; else refit minimally from raw data ------
if (!exists("cnorm_models")) {
  message("cnorm_models not found; attempting minimal refit from 'data_by_family' ...")
  stopifnot(exists("data_by_family"))  # fail early if no source data is available

  # -- Minimal helpers (mirror the C-Norm pipeline logic) -----------------------
  prep_family_df_cnorm <- function(df) {
    # If interval bounds are missing, fall back to exact counts as degenerate intervals
    if (!all(c("lower","upper") %in% names(df))) {
      if ("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(df)) {
        df$lower <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
        df$upper <- df$TOTAL_NUMBER
      } else if ("TOTAL_RND" %in% names(df)) {
        df$lower <- df$TOTAL_RND
        df$upper <- df$TOTAL_RND
      } else {
        stop("Need (lower, upper) or numeric TOTAL_NUMBER/TOTAL_RND.")
      }
    }
    if (!"SITE_F" %in% names(df)) df$SITE_F <- factor(df$SITE_ID)

    # Derive decimal_date if not present (using SAMPLE_DATE if available)
    if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
      if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
        df$decimal_date <- lubridate::decimal_date(as.Date(df$SAMPLE_DATE))
      } else {
        stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE.")
      }
    }

    # Derive Spring/Autumn season from calendar month
    m <- if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
      lubridate::month(df$SAMPLE_DATE)
    } else {
      lubridate::month(lubridate::date_decimal(df$decimal_date))
    }

    # Compute midpoint, interval width, √-response and stabilised weights
    df %>%
      mutate(
        season = dplyr::case_when(
          m %in% 3:5  ~ "Spring",
          m %in% 9:11 ~ "Autumn",
          TRUE        ~ NA_character_
        ),
        mid    = pmax((lower + upper)/2, 0),
        width  = pmax(upper - lower, 0),
        y_sqrt = sqrt(mid),
        w_raw  = 1/(width + 1),         # narrower intervals -> higher weight
        w      = w_raw / mean(w_raw, na.rm = TRUE) # centre weights around ~1
      ) %>%
      filter(!is.na(season)) %>%
      mutate(season = factor(season, levels = c("Spring","Autumn"))) %>%
      filter(is.finite(decimal_date), is.finite(y_sqrt), !is.na(SITE_F))
  }

  # Fit the √-Gaussian GAM with seasonal smooths and site random effect
  fit_cnorm_season <- function(df) {
    mgcv::bam(
      y_sqrt ~ season + s(decimal_date, by = season, k = 10) + s(SITE_F, bs = "re"),
      family   = gaussian(),   # identity link on √(mid)
      data     = df,
      weights  = df$w,         # weighting by interval precision
      method   = "fREML",
      discrete = TRUE
    )
  }

  # Families we care about (safeguard to known set)
  fams <- intersect(
    names(data_by_family),
    c("Aphelocheiridae","Brachycentridae","Cordulegastridae","Odontoceridae","Potamanthidae")
  )
  stopifnot(length(fams) > 0)

  # Attempt to prepare & fit each family; store into `cnorm_models`
  cnorm_models <- setNames(vector("list", length(fams)), fams)
  for (fam in fams) {
    df_raw <- data_by_family[[fam]]
    if (is.null(df_raw) || !nrow(df_raw)) next
    df <- try(prep_family_df_cnorm(df_raw), silent = TRUE)
    if (inherits(df, "try-error") || !nrow(df)) next
    mod <- try(fit_cnorm_season(df), silent = TRUE)
    if (!inherits(mod, "try-error")) {
      cnorm_models[[fam]] <- list(model = mod, data = df)
    }
  }
}

# Keep only families with a valid fitted model component
valid_fams <- names(cnorm_models)[vapply(cnorm_models, function(x) !is.null(x$model), logical(1))]
stopifnot(length(valid_fams) > 0)

# ----- Utility getters -----
# Extract a parametric term either as estimate or p-value from summary(model)
get_param <- function(summ, term, what = c("est","p")) {
  what <- match.arg(what)
  pt <- try(as.data.frame(summ$p.table), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(pt, "try-error") || is.null(pt) || !nrow(pt)) return(NA_real_)
  r <- rownames(pt)
  if (!(term %in% r)) return(NA_real_)
  if (what == "est") {
    suppressWarnings(as.numeric(pt[term, 1]))              # estimate column
  } else {
    pcol <- tail(colnames(pt), 1)                          # last column is p-value
    suppressWarnings(as.numeric(pt[term, pcol]))
  }
}

# Extract a smooth term’s edf or p-value (first matching row if multiple)
get_smooth_stat <- function(summ, smooth_label, what = c("edf","p")) {
  what <- match.arg(what)
  st <- try(as.data.frame(summ$s.table), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(st, "try-error") || is.null(st) || !nrow(st)) return(NA_real_)
  r <- rownames(st)
  idx <- which(r == smooth_label)
  if (!length(idx)) idx <- grep(smooth_label, r, fixed = TRUE)  # fallback to partial match
  if (!length(idx)) return(NA_real_)
  if (what == "edf") {
    suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st[idx[1], "edf"]))
  } else {
    suppressWarnings(as.numeric(st[idx[1], "p-value"]))
  }
}

# ---------- Build the compact, readable table (one row per family) ---------------
cnorm_compact <- lapply(valid_fams, function(fam) {
  mod <- cnorm_models[[fam]]$model
  s   <- summary(mod)

  # High-level fit metrics (robust to errors / unavailable values)
  n_rows   <- length(mod$y)
  aic_val  <- try(AIC(mod), silent = TRUE); if (inherits(aic_val, "try-error")) aic_val <- NA_real_
  r2_adj   <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(s$r.sq))          # adjusted R² on √-scale
  dev_pct  <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(s$dev.expl) * 100)# % deviance explained
  sigma2   <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(s$scale))         # residual variance on √-scale

  # Parametric effects (√-scale)
  b0_est   <- get_param(s, "(Intercept)", "est")
  b0_p     <- get_param(s, "(Intercept)", "p")
  bAut_est <- get_param(s, "seasonAutumn", "est")    # Autumn vs Spring contrast
  bAut_p   <- get_param(s, "seasonAutumn", "p")

  # Smooths: time-by-season and site random effect edf
  edf_spr  <- get_smooth_stat(s, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring", "edf")
  p_spr    <- get_smooth_stat(s, "s(decimal_date):seasonSpring", "p")
  edf_aut  <- get_smooth_stat(s, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn", "edf")
  p_aut    <- get_smooth_stat(s, "s(decimal_date):seasonAutumn", "p")
  edf_re   <- get_smooth_stat(s, "s(SITE_F)", "edf")

  tibble::tibble(
    Family = fam,
    `Samples(n)` = n_rows,
    `Adj. R² (√-scale)` = r2_adj,
    `Deviance explained (%)` = dev_pct,
    `AIC` = aic_val,
    `Scale (σ², √-scale)` = sigma2,
    `Intercept (√-scale)` = b0_est,
    `Intercept p` = b0_p,
    `Autumn vs Spring (√-scale)` = bAut_est,
    `Autumn vs Spring p` = bAut_p,
    `Time smooth edf — Spring` = edf_spr,
    `Time smooth p — Spring` = p_spr,
    `Time smooth edf — Autumn` = edf_aut,
    `Time smooth p — Autumn` = p_aut,
    `Site RE edf` = edf_re
  )
}) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows() %>%
  # Readability pass: round numeric columns (p-values with signif, others with round)
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~ ifelse(
          is.finite(.x),
          ifelse(grepl("p$", cur_column()), signif(.x, 3), round(.x, 3)),
          NA_real_
        )
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Family)

# Preview the compact table in the console
print(cnorm_compact)

```
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# National trend curves (site-marginal) — C-NORM models (√-Gaussian)
# ----------------------------------------------------------------
# Purpose
# • Produce national (population-level) trend curves per family and season
#   from the already-fitted C-NORM models stored in `cnorm_models`,
#   marginalising over site random effects.
# • Add 95% intervals and annotate key QC dates (1990, 1995, ~2000).
#
# How this matches your pipeline
# • Uses `cnorm_models[[fam]]$model` (mgcv::bam) and `$data` prepared by your
#   C-NORM ALL-FAMILIES chunk (which built `season`, `decimal_date`, `SITE_F`,
#   and fit on y = sqrt(mid)).
# • Predictions are made on the √-scale with `type = "link"` and the site
#   random effect excluded (via `exclude = "s(SITE_F)"`) to obtain *site-
#   marginal* population trends. We then back-transform by squaring.
# • Factor levels are taken from the model frame to avoid “new level” issues.
#
# Outputs
# • A tidy tibble `nat_df` with monthly national curves (Spring & Autumn) for
#   each family, with mean and 95% CI on the original abundance (count) scale.
# • A faceted ggplot of the national curves with QC vertical reference lines.
#
# Notes
# • CIs use (fit ± 1.96*SE)^2 on the √-scale; simple and conservative.
# • If `theme_ea` / `season_cols` are available (from your EDA setup chunk),
#   they are used; otherwise sensible fallbacks are applied.
# ================================================================

# ---------- Guardrails ----------
stopifnot(exists("cnorm_models"))
stopifnot(length(cnorm_models) > 0)

# ---------- Helper: build one family's national (site-marginal) curve ----------
# Inputs:
#   fam : family name (character, list name)
#   res : list with elements `model` (bam) and `data` (analysis frame)
# Returns:
#   tibble with SAMPLE_DATE (monthly grid), season, mean & 95% CI on count scale
nat_curve_one <- function(res, fam) {
  m  <- res$model    # fitted bam (C-NORM)
  df <- res$data     # data used to fit the model

  # Extract factor levels as used by the model to avoid level mismatches
  mf          <- stats::model.frame(m)
  site_levels <- levels(mf$SITE_F)
  seas_levels <- levels(mf$season)

  # Build a monthly date grid over the observed time span
  if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
    start_date <- min(df$SAMPLE_DATE, na.rm = TRUE)
    end_date   <- max(df$SAMPLE_DATE, na.rm = TRUE)
  } else {
    # If dates aren’t present, derive approximate dates from decimal years
    start_date <- as.Date(lubridate::date_decimal(min(df$decimal_date, na.rm = TRUE)))
    end_date   <- as.Date(lubridate::date_decimal(max(df$decimal_date, na.rm = TRUE)))
  }
  dates <- seq(start_date, end_date, by = "1 month")

  # Newdata grid: both seasons at each monthly date; a valid dummy SITE_F level
  newd <- tidyr::expand_grid(
    SAMPLE_DATE = dates,
    season      = factor(seas_levels, levels = seas_levels)
  ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      decimal_date = lubridate::decimal_date(SAMPLE_DATE),
      SITE_F       = factor(site_levels[1], levels = site_levels)  # any valid site level
    )

  # Identify the site random-effect smooth label(s) to exclude during prediction
  sm_labels <- vapply(m$smooth, function(s) s$label, character(1))
  sm_ex     <- sm_labels[grepl("^s\\(SITE_F", sm_labels)]
  if (!length(sm_ex)) sm_ex <- "s(SITE_F)"  # fallback to the usual label

  # Predict on √-scale (identity link): fit and SE, marginalising over site RE
  pr   <- predict(m, newdata = newd, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE, exclude = sm_ex)
  fitS <- pmax(as.numeric(pr$fit), 0)      # guard against tiny negatives
  seS  <- pmax(as.numeric(pr$se.fit), 0)

  # Back-transform to original (count) scale: μ = fit^2; CI via (fit ± 1.96*SE)^2
  mu <- fitS^2
  lo <- pmax(fitS - 1.96 * seS, 0)^2
  hi <- pmax(fitS + 1.96 * seS, 0)^2

  dplyr::mutate(newd,
                mu = mu, lo = lo, hi = hi,
                family = fam)
}

# ---------- Build curves for all families in `cnorm_models` ----------
# `imap_dfr` passes (.x = value, .y = name); our helper expects (res, fam)
nat_df <- purrr::imap_dfr(cnorm_models, nat_curve_one)

# ---------- Save tidy series to CSV ----------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
readr::write_csv(
  dplyr::select(nat_df,
                family,
                season,
                date = SAMPLE_DATE,
                mu_count = mu,
                lo_count = lo,
                hi_count = hi),
  "outputs/national_trends_site_marginal.csv"
)

# ---------- QC vertical reference lines ----------
vline_df <- tibble::tibble(
  x  = as.Date(c("1990-01-01","1995-01-01","2000-01-01")),
  lt = factor(c("1990 standardisation","1995 extra QC","~2000 method shift"),
              levels = c("1990 standardisation","1995 extra QC","~2000 method shift"))
)

# ---------- Plot ----------
# Use your EA theme / colours if available; otherwise sensible fallbacks
has_theme <- exists("theme_ea")
has_cols  <- exists("season_cols")

p_nat <- ggplot(nat_df, aes(SAMPLE_DATE, mu, colour = season, fill = season)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ family, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  geom_vline(
  data = vline_df,
  aes(xintercept = x, linetype = lt),
  linewidth = 0.4,
  colour = "black",          # <- fixes the need to disable inheritance
  show.legend = TRUE
) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("1990 standardisation" = 2,
                                   "1995 extra QC"      = 3,
                                   "~2000 method shift" = 3),
                        name = NULL) +
  labs(title    = "National abundance trends (site-marginal, C-NORM)",
       subtitle = "Spring vs Autumn; 95% CIs; verticals mark 1990/1995/~2000 QC milestones",
       x = NULL, y = "Estimated abundance (count scale)",
       colour = "Season", fill = "Season") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "5 years", date_labels = "%Y") +
  { if (has_cols) scale_colour_manual(values = season_cols) else ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") } +
  { if (has_cols) scale_fill_manual(values = season_cols)   else ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1") } +
  { if (has_theme) theme_ea(12) else theme_minimal(base_size = 12) } +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_nat)

```


```{r, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
# ============================================================
# Maps & change summaries for C-NORM pipeline (NO final_results)
# Works with: cnorm_models[[fam]] = list(model=<bam>, data=<df>)
# Coordinates from: sites_clean / metrics_clean / any cnorm_models$data / data_by_family
# Presence maps: models_data[[fam]]$pa OR data_by_family[[fam]]
# ============================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(tibble); library(purrr)
  library(lubridate); library(ggplot2); library(scales)
  library(sf); library(rnaturalearth); library(MASS)
})

`%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b

# -------------------------- robust helpers --------------------------

.first_col <- function(df, candidates) {
  hit <- intersect(candidates, names(df)); if (length(hit)) hit[1] else NA_character_
}

.site_factor_name <- function(fit) {
  mf <- stats::model.frame(fit)
  pick <- intersect(c("SITE_F","SITE_ID.F","site_f","site"), names(mf))
  if (!length(pick)) stop("Site random-effect factor not found in model frame.")
  pick[1]
}

.season_col_name <- function(df) {
  nm <- .first_col(df, c("season_f","season"))
  if (is.na(nm)) stop("Need a season column named 'season_f' or 'season' in res$data.")
  nm
}

.ensure_decimal_date <- function(df) {
  if (!"decimal_date" %in% names(df)) {
    if ("SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
      df <- dplyr::mutate(df, SAMPLE_DATE = as.Date(SAMPLE_DATE),
                          decimal_date = lubridate::decimal_date(SAMPLE_DATE))
    } else stop("Need decimal_date or SAMPLE_DATE in res$data.")
  }
  df
}

# site lon/lat from a generic coordinates table (accepts lon/lat OR BNG)
.sites_lonlat_from <- function(x) {
  stopifnot("SITE_ID" %in% names(x))
  # Prefer lon/lat if present
  if (all(c("lon","lat") %in% names(x))) {
    return(x %>%
             dplyr::select(SITE_ID, lon, lat) %>%
             dplyr::distinct() %>%
             dplyr::filter(is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat)))
  }
  # Otherwise try common BNG column names
  east_nm  <- .first_col(x, c("FULL_EASTING","EASTING","X","easting","EAST"))
  north_nm <- .first_col(x, c("FULL_NORTHING","NORTHING","Y","northing","NORTH"))
  if (is.na(east_nm) || is.na(north_nm))
    stop("Coordinate table must have lon/lat or BNG easting/northing.")
  x %>%
    dplyr::select(SITE_ID, e = dplyr::all_of(east_nm), n = dplyr::all_of(north_nm)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(e), is.finite(n)) %>%
    sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("e","n"), crs = 27700) %>%
    sf::st_transform(4326) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(lon = sf::st_coordinates(geometry)[,1],
                  lat = sf::st_coordinates(geometry)[,2]) %>%
    sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
    dplyr::select(SITE_ID, lon, lat) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
}

# Try multiple sources to build site lon/lat: sites_clean, metrics_clean,
# any cnorm_models$data (or data_by_family as a last resort)
.sites_lonlat_any <- function() {
  # 1) sites_clean (preferred)
  if (exists("sites_clean", inherits = TRUE) && is.data.frame(get("sites_clean", inherits = TRUE))) {
    out <- try(.sites_lonlat_from(get("sites_clean", inherits = TRUE)), silent = TRUE)
    if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)
  }
  # 2) metrics_clean
  if (exists("metrics_clean", inherits = TRUE) && is.data.frame(get("metrics_clean", inherits = TRUE))) {
    out <- try(.sites_lonlat_from(get("metrics_clean", inherits = TRUE)), silent = TRUE)
    if (!inherits(out, "try-error")) return(out)
  }
  # 3) look inside cnorm_models
  if (exists("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE) && length(get("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE))) {
    cm <- get("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE)
    for (nm in names(cm)) {
      d <- cm[[nm]]$data
      if (is.null(d) || !"SITE_ID" %in% names(d)) next
      if (all(c("lon","lat") %in% names(d))) return(.sites_lonlat_from(d))
      if (!is.na(.first_col(d, c("FULL_EASTING","EASTING","X","easting","EAST"))) &&
          !is.na(.first_col(d, c("FULL_NORTHING","NORTHING","Y","northing","NORTH")))) {
        return(.sites_lonlat_from(d))
      }
    }
  }
  # 4) last resort: any data_by_family that carries coords
  if (exists("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE) && length(get("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE))) {
    dbf <- get("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE)
    for (nm in names(dbf)) {
      d <- dbf[[nm]]
      if (is.null(d) || !"SITE_ID" %in% names(d)) next
      if (all(c("lon","lat") %in% names(d))) return(.sites_lonlat_from(d))
      if (!is.na(.first_col(d, c("FULL_EASTING","EASTING","X","easting","EAST"))) &&
          !is.na(.first_col(d, c("FULL_NORTHING","NORTHING","Y","northing","NORTH")))) {
        return(.sites_lonlat_from(d))
      }
    }
  }
  stop("Could not find site coordinates. Provide `sites_clean` (lon/lat or BNG), ",
       "`metrics_clean`, or include coords in cnorm_models[[*]]$data / data_by_family.")
}

# Lazy/memoized cache for site lon/lat
.SITES_LL_cache <- NULL
.SITES_LL <- function() {
  if (!is.null(.SITES_LL_cache)) return(.SITES_LL_cache)
  .SITES_LL_cache <<- .sites_lonlat_any()
  .SITES_LL_cache
}

# presence-table getter: models_data[[fam]]$pa OR data_by_family[[fam]]
.get_pa_table <- function(fam) {
  if (exists("models_data", inherits = TRUE) && fam %in% names(get("models_data", inherits = TRUE))) {
    md <- get("models_data", inherits = TRUE)
    if ("pa" %in% names(md[[fam]])) {
      pa <- md[[fam]]$pa
    } else {
      stop("models_data[['", fam, "']] exists but has no $pa table.")
    }
  } else if (exists("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE) && fam %in% names(get("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE))) {
    pa <- get("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE)[[fam]]
  } else {
    stop("Neither models_data[['", fam, "']]$pa nor data_by_family[['", fam, "']] is available.")
  }

  # Ensure SITE_ID & date
  stopifnot("SITE_ID" %in% names(pa))
  if (!"SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(pa)) {
    if ("decimal_date" %in% names(pa)) {
      pa$SAMPLE_DATE <- as.Date(lubridate::date_decimal(pa$decimal_date))
    } else stop("Presence table needs SAMPLE_DATE or decimal_date.")
  } else {
    pa$SAMPLE_DATE <- as.Date(pa$SAMPLE_DATE)
  }

  # Presence flag
  pres_nm <- .first_col(pa, c("taxon_present","present"))
  if (is.na(pres_nm)) {
    if ("TOTAL_NUMBER" %in% names(pa)) {
      pa$present <- pa$TOTAL_NUMBER > 0; pres_nm <- "present"
    } else if ("TOTAL_RND" %in% names(pa)) {
      pa$present <- pa$TOTAL_RND > 0;    pres_nm <- "present"
    } else stop("Cannot derive presence (need taxon_present/present or counts).")
  }
  pa %>% dplyr::select(SITE_ID, SAMPLE_DATE, !!pres_nm)
}

.uk_sf <- function() {
  rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
    dplyr::filter(admin %in% c("United Kingdom","Ireland")) %>% sf::st_transform(4326)
}

posterior_mu <- function(fit, newdata, nsim = 400, exclude = NULL, seed = 123) {
  X <- predict(fit, newdata = newdata, type = "lpmatrix", exclude = exclude)
  b <- coef(fit); V <- vcov(fit, unconditional = FALSE)
  set.seed(seed); B <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = nsim, mu = b, Sigma = V)
  as.matrix(B %*% t(X))   # nsim x n_new
}

# Back-transform from model response to count-scale index (cNORM sqrt ⇒ p=2)
bt_idx <- function(x, p) pmax(0, x)^p

# ---------------- input resolver (NO final_results) -------------------

.site_trend_input <- function(fam_name = NULL, res = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(res)) {
    # Ensure we have a family name for labelling
    if (is.null(res$family)) res$family <- fam_name %||% "cNORM family"
    if (is.null(res$p))      res$p      <- 2
    return(res)
  }
  if (is.null(fam_name)) stop("Provide either `fam_name` or `res`.")
  if (!exists("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE))
    stop("`cnorm_models` not found. Fit models first, or pass `res = cnorm_models[['", fam_name, "']]`.")
  cm <- get("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE)
  if (!fam_name %in% names(cm))
    stop("Family '", fam_name, "' not in `cnorm_models`. Available: ", paste(names(cm), collapse = ", "))
  out <- cm[[fam_name]] %||% stop("Empty entry for '", fam_name, "' in cnorm_models.")
  out$family <- out$family %||% fam_name
  out$p      <- out$p %||% 2
  out
}

# ---------------- 1) UK map — site-level % change with certainty --------------

site_trend_with_sig <- function(res, early_year_max = 2005, nsim = 400) {
  fit <- res$model; df <- res$data; fam <- res$family; pwr <- res$p %||% 2
  df <- .ensure_decimal_date(df)
  stopifnot(all(c("SITE_ID","SAMPLE_DATE") %in% names(df)))

  site_fac <- .site_factor_name(fit); if (!site_fac %in% names(df)) df[[site_fac]] <- factor(df$SITE_ID)
  seas_col <- .season_col_name(df);   seas_lvls <- levels(df[[seas_col]])

  site_period <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(period = dplyr::if_else(lubridate::year(SAMPLE_DATE) <= early_year_max, "early", "recent")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID, period) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(dec = mean(decimal_date), n = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = c(dec, n)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(dec_early), !is.na(dec_recent))

  if (!nrow(site_period)) return(tibble())

  base_grid <- tidyr::expand_grid(SITE_ID = site_period$SITE_ID, season_tmp = seas_lvls) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      !!site_fac := factor(SITE_ID, levels = levels(df[[site_fac]])),
      !!seas_col := factor(season_tmp, levels = seas_lvls)
    ) %>% dplyr::select(-season_tmp)

  new_early  <- dplyr::left_join(base_grid, site_period, by = "SITE_ID") %>% dplyr::mutate(decimal_date = dec_early)
  new_recent <- dplyr::left_join(base_grid, site_period, by = "SITE_ID") %>% dplyr::mutate(decimal_date = dec_recent)

  De <- posterior_mu(fit, new_early,  nsim = nsim, exclude = NULL, seed = 2025)
  Dr <- posterior_mu(fit, new_recent, nsim = nsim, exclude = NULL, seed = 2026)

  n_seas <- length(seas_lvls)
  split_cols <- split(seq_len(ncol(De)), rep(seq_along(site_period$SITE_ID), each = n_seas))
  E_mean <- sapply(split_cols, function(ix) rowMeans(De[, ix, drop = FALSE]))
  R_mean <- sapply(split_cols, function(ix) rowMeans(Dr[, ix, drop = FALSE]))

  diff_p   <- R_mean - E_mean
  prob_inc <- colMeans(diff_p > 0)
  mu_e     <- colMeans(E_mean)
  mu_r     <- colMeans(R_mean)

  early_count  <- bt_idx(mu_e, pwr)
  recent_count <- bt_idx(mu_r, pwr)
  delta_pct    <- (recent_count / pmax(early_count, 1e-9)) - 1

  tibble(
    SITE_ID   = site_period$SITE_ID,
    delta_pct = delta_pct,
    prob_inc  = prob_inc,
    n_total   = site_period$n_early + site_period$n_recent
  ) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(.SITES_LL(), by = "SITE_ID") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      size_sqrt = sqrt(pmax(n_total, 1)),
      sig       = dplyr::case_when(prob_inc >= 0.8 ~ "increase",
                                   prob_inc <= 0.2 ~ "decrease",
                                   TRUE            ~ "uncertain"),
      family    = fam
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(delta_pct), is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat))
}

plot_trend_map_one <- function(fam_name = NULL,
                               res = NULL,
                               early_year_max = 2005,
                               bbox = c(xmin = -6, xmax = 2.2, ymin = 50, ymax = 56.5)) {
  res <- .site_trend_input(fam_name = fam_name, res = res)
  pts <- site_trend_with_sig(res, early_year_max = early_year_max)
  uk  <- .uk_sf()

  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = uk, fill = "grey90", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_point(data = pts,
               aes(lon, lat, colour = delta_pct, alpha = sig, size = size_sqrt),
               stroke = 0, show.legend = TRUE) +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox["xmin"], bbox["xmax"]), ylim = c(bbox["ymin"], bbox["ymax"]), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_colour_gradient2(low = "#2C7FB8", mid = "#FEE08B", high = "#D7191C",
                           midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1),
                           labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
                           name = "% change (recent vs early)") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c(decrease = 1, increase = 1, uncertain = 0.35),
                       name = "Certainty",
                       labels = c("decrease (Pr≤0.2)", "increase (Pr≥0.8)", "uncertain")) +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "sqrt(total samples)", range = c(1.2, 4.2),
                          breaks = sqrt(c(1, 2, 4, 9)), labels = c("1","2","4","9")) +
    labs(title = paste0("Site-level % change — ", res$family %||% fam_name),
         subtitle = "cNORM predictions (site REs included). Early ≤ 2005 vs Recent ≥ 2006.",
         x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), legend.position = "right")
}

# --------------- 2) Early vs recent abundance maps (+ change) -----------------

abundance_maps <- function(fam_name = NULL, res = NULL, early_year_max = 2005,
                           bbox = c(xmin = -6.5, xmax = 2.5, ymin = 50, ymax = 56.5)) {
  res <- .site_trend_input(fam_name = fam_name, res = res)
  fit <- res$model; df <- .ensure_decimal_date(res$data); pwr <- res$p %||% 2
  stopifnot(all(c("SITE_ID","SAMPLE_DATE") %in% names(df)))

  site_fac <- .site_factor_name(fit); if (!site_fac %in% names(df)) df[[site_fac]] <- factor(df$SITE_ID)
  seas_col <- .season_col_name(df);   seas_lvls <- levels(df[[seas_col]])

  site_t <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(period = dplyr::if_else(lubridate::year(SAMPLE_DATE) <= early_year_max, "early","recent")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID, period) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(dec = mean(decimal_date), .groups = "drop") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = dec) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(early), !is.na(recent))

  base <- tidyr::expand_grid(SITE_ID = site_t$SITE_ID, season_tmp = seas_lvls) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      !!site_fac := factor(SITE_ID, levels = levels(df[[site_fac]])),
      !!seas_col := factor(season_tmp, levels = seas_lvls)
    ) %>% dplyr::select(-season_tmp)

  new_early  <- dplyr::left_join(base, site_t, by = "SITE_ID") %>% dplyr::mutate(decimal_date = early)
  new_recent <- dplyr::left_join(base, site_t, by = "SITE_ID") %>% dplyr::mutate(decimal_date = recent)

  mu_e <- as.numeric(predict(fit, newdata = new_early,  type = "response"))
  mu_r <- as.numeric(predict(fit, newdata = new_recent, type = "response"))

  n_seas <- length(seas_lvls)
  by_site <- function(v) sapply(split(seq_along(v), rep(seq_along(site_t$SITE_ID), each = n_seas)),
                                function(ix) mean(v[ix]))
  early_mean_p  <- by_site(mu_e)
  recent_mean_p <- by_site(mu_r)

  site_mu <- tibble(
    SITE_ID = site_t$SITE_ID,
    Early   = bt_idx(early_mean_p,  pwr),
    Recent  = bt_idx(recent_mean_p, pwr)
  ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(delta = Recent - Early) %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(.SITES_LL(), by = "SITE_ID")

  lims <- stats::quantile(c(site_mu$Early, site_mu$Recent), c(.05, .95), na.rm = TRUE)
  D    <- stats::quantile(abs(site_mu$delta), .95, na.rm = TRUE)
  uk   <- .uk_sf()

  base_layers <- list(
    ggplot2::geom_sf(data = uk, fill = "grey95", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3),
    ggplot2::coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox["xmin"], bbox["xmax"]), ylim = c(bbox["ymin"], bbox["ymax"]), expand = FALSE),
    ggplot2::theme(panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.2), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  )

  p_early <- ggplot() + base_layers +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = site_mu, aes(lon, lat, colour = Early), size = 1.8, alpha = .9) +
    ggplot2::scale_colour_viridis_c(limits = lims, name = "Mean abundance\n(index)") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = paste("Early (≤", early_year_max, ") —", res$family %||% fam_name))

  p_recent <- ggplot() + base_layers +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = site_mu, aes(lon, lat, colour = Recent), size = 1.8, alpha = .9) +
    ggplot2::scale_colour_viridis_c(limits = lims, name = "Mean abundance\n(index)") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = paste("Recent (≥", early_year_max + 1, ") —", res$family %||% fam_name))

  p_change <- ggplot() + base_layers +
    ggplot2::geom_point(data = site_mu, aes(lon, lat, colour = delta), size = 2.0, alpha = .95) +
    ggplot2::scale_colour_gradient2(low = "#355C7D", mid = "#F8E694", high = "#C02942",
                                    midpoint = 0, limits = c(-D, D), name = "Δ abundance") +
    ggplot2::labs(title = paste("Change (recent − early) —", res$family %||% fam_name))

  list(early = p_early, recent = p_recent, change = p_change)
}

# -------------------------- 3) Presence maps (data-driven) --------------------

presence_2win <- function(fam, early = 1990:1994, recent = 2020:2024) {
  pa <- .get_pa_table(fam)
  pres_nm <- .first_col(pa, c("taxon_present","present"))
  pts <- pa %>%
    dplyr::mutate(year = lubridate::year(SAMPLE_DATE),
                  period = dplyr::case_when(year %in% early ~ "Early",
                                            year %in% recent ~ "Recent")) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(period)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID, period) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(pres = mean(.data[[pres_nm]]), n = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(.SITES_LL(), by = "SITE_ID") %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat))
  uk <- .uk_sf()
  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = uk, fill = "grey95", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.25) +
    geom_point(data = pts, aes(lon, lat, colour = pres, size = sqrt(pmax(n,1))), alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_colour_viridis_c(limits = c(0,1), labels = scales::percent, name = "Presence") +
    scale_size(range = c(0.6, 4), name = "√ samples") +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-6, 2.2), ylim = c(49.5, 56.7), expand = FALSE) +
    facet_wrap(~ period) +
    labs(title = paste("Presence of", fam, "— early vs recent"),
         subtitle = "Colour = presence rate; size = √(samples at site)") +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
}

presence_change_map <- function(fam, early = c(1988, 1992), late = c(2023, 2027)) {
  pa <- .get_pa_table(fam)
  pres_nm <- .first_col(pa, c("taxon_present","present"))

  early_df <- pa %>%
    dplyr::filter(SAMPLE_DATE >= as.Date(sprintf("%d-01-01", early[1])),
                  SAMPLE_DATE <= as.Date(sprintf("%d-12-31", early[2]))) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(p_early = mean(.data[[pres_nm]]), n_early = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop")

  late_df  <- pa %>%
    dplyr::filter(SAMPLE_DATE >= as.Date(sprintf("%d-01-01", late[1])),
                  SAMPLE_DATE <= as.Date(sprintf("%d-12-31", late[2]))) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(p_late = mean(.data[[pres_nm]]), n_late = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop")

  dd <- dplyr::inner_join(early_df, late_df, by = "SITE_ID") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(delta = p_late - p_early, n_tot = n_early + n_late, size_sqrt = sqrt(pmax(n_tot, 1))) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(.SITES_LL(), by = "SITE_ID") %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat))

  uk <- .uk_sf()
  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = uk, fill = "grey97", colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_point(data = dd, aes(lon, lat, colour = delta, size = size_sqrt), alpha = 0.9) +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-6, 2.2), ylim = c(50, 56.5), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_colour_gradient2(low = "#355C7D", mid = "#F8E694", high = "#C02942",
                           midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), labels = scales::percent, name = "Δ presence") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(1.2, 4.2), name = "√ total samples") +
    labs(title = sprintf("Change in presence — %s", fam),
         subtitle = sprintf("Early: %d–%d vs Late: %d–%d", early[1], early[2], late[1], late[2]))
}

presence_window <- function(fam, year, window = 2) {
  pa <- .get_pa_table(fam)
  pres_nm <- .first_col(pa, c("taxon_present","present"))
  rng <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-01-01", year + c(-window, window)))
  pa %>%
    dplyr::select(SITE_ID, SAMPLE_DATE, !!pres_nm) %>%
    dplyr::filter(SAMPLE_DATE >= rng[1], SAMPLE_DATE <= rng[2]) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(n = dplyr::n(), presence = mean(.data[[pres_nm]]), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(.SITES_LL(), by = "SITE_ID") %>%
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(size_sqrt = sqrt(pmax(n, 1)))
}

plot_presence_year <- function(df_year, fam, year, window = 2) {
  uk <- .uk_sf()
  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = uk, fill = "grey97", colour = "grey75", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_point(data = df_year, aes(lon, lat, colour = presence, size = size_sqrt), alpha = 0.9) +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-6, 2.2), ylim = c(50, 56.5), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_colour_viridis_c(limits = c(0,1), labels = scales::percent, name = "Presence") +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(1.2, 4.2), name = "√ samples") +
    labs(title = sprintf("Presence of %s around %d (±%dy)", fam, year, window)) +
    theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
}

# ----------------------------- examples --------------------------------
# Use EITHER fam_name (looked up in cnorm_models) OR pass res = cnorm_models[[fam]]

fam <- "Aphelocheiridae"

# 1) UK map — site-level % change with certainty (uses cnorm_models)
if (exists("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE) && fam %in% names(get("cnorm_models", inherits = TRUE))) {
  print(plot_trend_map_one(fam_name = fam))  # or: print(plot_trend_map_one(res = cnorm_models[[fam]], fam_name = fam))

  # 2) Early vs recent abundance maps (+ change)
  ab <- abundance_maps(fam_name = fam)       # or: ab <- abundance_maps(res = cnorm_models[[fam]], fam_name = fam)
  print(ab$early); print(ab$recent); print(ab$change)
} else {
  message("`cnorm_models` not found or family '", fam, "' missing. Fit C-NORM models first.")
}

# 3) Presence maps (work with models_data or data_by_family)
if (exists("models_data", inherits = TRUE) || exists("data_by_family", inherits = TRUE)) {
  print(presence_2win(fam))
  print(presence_change_map(fam))
  dfy <- presence_window(fam, year = 2005, window = 2)
  print(plot_presence_year(dfy, fam, 2005, window = 2))
} else {
  message("No models_data/data_by_family detected — skipping presence map examples.")
}


print(plot_trend_map_one(fam_name = "Aphelocheiridae"))
ab <- abundance_maps(fam_name = "Aphelocheiridae")

```
```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# ================================================================
# EA-ready map: Site-level % change with certainty (cNORM models)
# - Works with final_results[[fam]] OR cnorm_models[[fam]]
# - Uses site random effects (INcluded) and season-averaged predictions
# - Colour = % change (recent vs early, clamped to ±100%)
# - Alpha  = certainty (Pr↑≥0.8 / Pr↓≤0.2), Size = sqrt(total samples)
# - Robust lon/lat: uses lon/lat if present, else converts BNG → WGS84
#   (falls back across: model data → sites_clean → metrics_clean)
# ================================================================

# ---- basemap helper (cached) ----
.uk_sf <- local({
  cache <- NULL
  function() {
    if (is.null(cache)) {
      cache <<- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") |>
        dplyr::filter(admin %in% c("United Kingdom","Ireland")) |>
        sf::st_transform(4326)
    }
    cache
  }
})

# ---- sites → lon/lat (robust for any input df) ----
sites_lonlat <- function(sites_df) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(sites_df), "SITE_ID" %in% names(sites_df))
  nms <- names(sites_df)

  # 1) If lon/lat already present, use them (accept common name variants)
  lon_name <- intersect(c("lon","Lon","LONGITUDE","Longitude","longitude"), nms)
  lat_name <- intersect(c("lat","Lat","LATITUDE","Latitude","latitude"), nms)
  if (length(lon_name) >= 1 && length(lat_name) >= 1) {
    lon_name <- lon_name[1]; lat_name <- lat_name[1]
    out <- sites_df |>
      dplyr::select(SITE_ID,
                    lon = dplyr::all_of(lon_name),
                    lat = dplyr::all_of(lat_name)) |>
      dplyr::filter(is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat)) |>
      dplyr::distinct()
    return(out)
  }

  # 2) Otherwise try to transform BNG → WGS84 (prefers FULL_EASTING/NORTHING)
  e_names <- c("FULL_EASTING","EASTING","X","easting","EAST")
  n_names <- c("FULL_NORTHING","NORTHING","Y","northing","NORTH")
  e_col <- intersect(e_names, nms)
  n_col <- intersect(n_names, nms)

  if (length(e_col) >= 1 && length(n_col) >= 1) {
    e_col <- e_col[1]; n_col <- n_col[1]
    out <- sites_df |>
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[e_col]]), !is.na(.data[[n_col]])) |>
      dplyr::select(SITE_ID,
                    e = dplyr::all_of(e_col),
                    n = dplyr::all_of(n_col)) |>
      sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("e","n"), crs = 27700, remove = FALSE) |>
      sf::st_transform(4326) |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        lon = sf::st_coordinates(geometry)[,1],
        lat = sf::st_coordinates(geometry)[,2]
      ) |>
      sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
      dplyr::select(SITE_ID, lon, lat) |>
      dplyr::distinct()
    return(out)
  }

  stop("sites_lonlat(): Could not find lon/lat or BNG easting/northing columns in the provided table.")
}

# ---- try multiple sources for coordinates (model data → sites_clean → metrics_clean) ----
sites_lonlat_any <- function(model_data = NULL) {
  sources <- list(model_data)
  if (exists("sites_clean", inherits = TRUE))   sources <- c(sources, list(get("sites_clean", inherits = TRUE)))
  if (exists("metrics_clean", inherits = TRUE)) sources <- c(sources, list(get("metrics_clean", inherits = TRUE)))

  for (src in sources) {
    if (is.null(src) || !is.data.frame(src)) next
    out <- try(sites_lonlat(src), silent = TRUE)
    if (!inherits(out, "try-error") && nrow(out)) return(out)
  }
  stop("No usable site coordinates found. Provide either:\n",
       "  • lon/lat columns (e.g., 'lon','lat'), or\n",
       "  • BNG columns (e.g., 'FULL_EASTING','FULL_NORTHING')\n",
       "in either the model data, `sites_clean`, or `metrics_clean`.")
}

# ---- pull one family's cNORM result (final_results OR cnorm_models) ----
get_cnorm_res <- function(fam) {
  if (exists("final_results") && fam %in% names(final_results)) {
    res <- final_results[[fam]]
    stopifnot(all(c("model","data") %in% names(res)))
    if (is.null(res$p)) res$p <- 2  # default to √-Gaussian back-transform
    if (is.null(res$family)) res$family <- fam
    return(res)
  }
  if (exists("cnorm_models") && fam %in% names(cnorm_models)) {
    obj <- cnorm_models[[fam]]
    stopifnot(all(c("model","data") %in% names(obj)))
    return(list(model = obj$model, data = obj$data, p = 2, family = fam))
  }
  stop("get_cnorm_res(): No cNORM result found for family '", fam, "'.")
}

# ---- posterior draws on p-scale (INCLUDES site REs) ----
posterior_mu <- function(fit, newdata, nsim = 600, seed = 123) {
  X <- predict(fit, newdata = newdata, type = "lpmatrix")
  b <- coef(fit)
  V <- vcov(fit, unconditional = FALSE)
  set.seed(seed)
  B <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = nsim, mu = b, Sigma = V)     # nsim x coef
  as.matrix(B %*% t(X))                               # nsim x n_new  (p-scale)
}

# ---- per-site early vs recent change with significance ----
# Returns: SITE_ID, lon, lat, size_sqrt, delta_pct, sig ∈ {increase,decrease,uncertain}, family
site_trend_with_sig <- function(fam, early_year_max = 2005, nsim = 600) {
  res <- get_cnorm_res(fam)
  fit <- res$model
  df  <- res$data
  pwr <- res$p

  # Identify column names as used in the fitted model
  mf <- stats::model.frame(fit)
  site_fac_name <- if ("SITE_ID.F" %in% names(mf)) "SITE_ID.F" else if ("SITE_F" %in% names(mf)) "SITE_F" else stop("Model is missing a site factor.")
  season_name   <- if ("season_f" %in% names(mf)) "season_f" else if ("season" %in% names(mf)) "season" else stop("Model is missing a season factor.")

  # Sample dates → period means per site
  if (!"SAMPLE_DATE" %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Data must contain SAMPLE_DATE to compute early/recent periods.")
  }
  stopifnot("decimal_date" %in% names(df), "SITE_ID" %in% names(df))

  site_period <- df |>
    dplyr::mutate(period = dplyr::if_else(lubridate::year(SAMPLE_DATE) <= early_year_max, "early","recent")) |>
    dplyr::group_by(SITE_ID, period) |>
    dplyr::summarise(dec = mean(decimal_date), n = dplyr::n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = c(dec, n)) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(dec_early), !is.na(dec_recent))

  # Build prediction grids for early & recent at each site, both seasons
  seas_levels <- levels(mf[[season_name]])
  site_levels <- levels(mf[[site_fac_name]])

  base_grid <- tidyr::expand_grid(
    SITE_ID         = site_period$SITE_ID,
    !!site_fac_name := factor(site_period$SITE_ID, levels = site_levels),
    !!season_name   := factor(seas_levels, levels = seas_levels)
  )

  new_early  <- dplyr::left_join(base_grid, site_period, by = "SITE_ID") |>
    dplyr::mutate(decimal_date = dec_early) |>
    dplyr::select(-starts_with("dec_"), -starts_with("n_"))
  new_recent <- dplyr::left_join(base_grid, site_period, by = "SITE_ID") |>
    dplyr::mutate(decimal_date = dec_recent) |>
    dplyr::select(-starts_with("dec_"), -starts_with("n_"))

  # Posterior draws (p-scale), then average across seasons per site in each draw
  D_e <- posterior_mu(fit, new_early,  nsim = nsim, seed = 2025)
  D_r <- posterior_mu(fit, new_recent, nsim = nsim, seed = 2026)

  n_seasons <- length(seas_levels)
  n_sites   <- dplyr::n_distinct(base_grid$SITE_ID)

  # columns are grouped by site * season; average seasons per site within each draw
  col_groups <- split(seq_len(ncol(D_e)), rep(seq_len(n_sites), each = n_seasons))
  E_mean <- sapply(col_groups, function(cols) rowMeans(D_e[, cols, drop = FALSE]))  # nsim x sites
  R_mean <- sapply(col_groups, function(cols) rowMeans(D_r[, cols, drop = FALSE]))  # nsim x sites

  # change on p-scale → probabilities of increase/decrease
  diff_p   <- R_mean - E_mean                      # nsim x sites (p-scale)
  prob_inc <- colMeans(diff_p > 0)
  prob_dec <- colMeans(diff_p < 0)

  # posterior means (p-scale) for back-transform to a count-scale index
  mu_e <- colMeans(E_mean)
  mu_r <- colMeans(R_mean)

  back <- function(x) pmax(0, x)^(1 / pwr)
  early_count  <- back(mu_e)
  recent_count <- back(mu_r)

  delta_pct <- (recent_count / pmax(early_count, 1e-9)) - 1  # fractional change

  # join coordinates & sample counts for symbol sizing
  site_n <- site_period |>
    dplyr::transmute(SITE_ID, n_total = n_early + n_recent)

  # <<< FIX >>> robust coordinate sourcing (model data → sites_clean → metrics_clean)
  coords <- sites_lonlat_any(model_data = df)

  tibble::tibble(
    SITE_ID   = unique(base_grid$SITE_ID),
    early     = mu_e,
    recent    = mu_r,
    delta_pct = delta_pct,
    prob_inc  = prob_inc,
    prob_dec  = prob_dec
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(site_n, by = "SITE_ID") |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      size_sqrt = sqrt(pmax(n_total, 1)),
      sig = dplyr::case_when(
        prob_inc >= 0.8 ~ "increase",
        prob_dec >= 0.8 ~ "decrease",
        TRUE            ~ "uncertain"
      ),
      family = res$family
    ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(coords, by = "SITE_ID") |>
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(delta_pct), is.finite(lon), is.finite(lat))
}

# ---- pretty map (appealing colours, concise caption) ----
plot_trend_map_pretty <- function(fam_name, early_year_max = 2005,
                                  bbox = c(xmin = -6, xmax = 2.2, ymin = 50, ymax = 56.5),
                                  nsim = 600) {

  uk  <- .uk_sf()
  pts <- site_trend_with_sig(fam_name, early_year_max = early_year_max, nsim = nsim)

  # caption stats
  n_sites <- nrow(pts)
  sh_inc  <- mean(pts$delta_pct >  0, na.rm = TRUE)
  sh_dec  <- mean(pts$delta_pct <  0, na.rm = TRUE)
  sh_unc  <- mean(pts$sig == "uncertain", na.rm = TRUE)
  cap_txt <- sprintf("Sites: %d  |  ↑: %s  ↓: %s  uncertain: %s  |  Early ≤ %d vs Recent ≥ %d",
                     n_sites,
                     scales::percent(sh_inc, 1),
                     scales::percent(sh_dec, 1),
                     scales::percent(sh_unc, 1),
                     early_year_max, early_year_max + 1)

  # diverging palette with warm (non-white) midpoint
  pal_low  <- "#2C7FB8"  # blue
  pal_mid  <- "#F6D878"  # honey/sand
  pal_high <- "#D7191C"  # red

  ggplot() +
    geom_sf(data = uk, fill = "grey92", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.35) +
    geom_point(
      data = pts,
      aes(lon, lat, fill = pmax(-1, pmin(1, delta_pct)), alpha = sig, size = size_sqrt),
      shape = 21, colour = "grey20", stroke = 0.25, show.legend = TRUE
    ) +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(bbox["xmin"], bbox["xmax"]),
             ylim = c(bbox["ymin"], bbox["ymax"]), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      low = pal_low, mid = pal_mid, high = pal_high,
      midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1),
      labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
      name   = "% change (recent vs early)"
    ) +
    scale_alpha_manual(
      values = c(decrease = 1, increase = 1, uncertain = 0.35),
      name   = "Certainty",
      labels = c("decrease (Pr≤0.2)", "increase (Pr≥0.8)", "uncertain")
    ) +
    scale_size_continuous(
      name   = "sqrt(total samples)",
      range  = c(1.4, 4.6),
      breaks = sqrt(c(1, 2, 4, 9)),
      labels = c("1","2","4","9")
    ) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Site-level % change — ", fam_name),
      subtitle = "cNORM predictions (site REs included); season-averaged",
      caption  = cap_txt,
      x = NULL, y = NULL
    ) +
    guides(
      fill  = guide_colourbar(order = 1, barheight = unit(60, "pt")),
      alpha = guide_legend(order = 2),
      size  = guide_legend(order = 3)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      panel.grid        = element_blank(),
      legend.position   = "right",
      legend.title      = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.key.height = unit(0.55, "lines"),
      plot.subtitle     = element_text(margin = margin(b = 6)),
      plot.caption      = element_text(hjust = 0, colour = "grey30", margin = margin(t = 8))
    )
}

# -------------------------
# EXAMPLE (prints the plot)
# -------------------------
# Requirements: EITHER final_results OR cnorm_models available from cNORM pipeline.
fam <- "Aphelocheiridae"
print(plot_trend_map_pretty(fam))


```
